1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
5	REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
6	PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
7	NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="${NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS:-16}"
8	TP_SIZE="${TP_SIZE:-8}"
9	MAX_MODEL_LEN="${MAX_MODEL_LEN:-128000}"
10	MAX_NUM_SEQS="${MAX_NUM_SEQS:-2}"
11	COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS="${COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS:-16384}"
12	COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS="${COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS:-114688}"
13	COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS="${COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS:-16384,32768,65536,98304,128000}"
14	OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="${OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG:-}"
15	NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG="${NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG:-2}"
16	NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH:-1750G}"
17	NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX:-1900G}"
18	NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE="${NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE:-1}"
19	BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR="${BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compile-work/tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8}"
20	NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL="${NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compile-cache/tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8}"
21	NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="${NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES:-0-7}"
22	COMPILE_LOCK_FILE="${COMPILE_LOCK_FILE:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/.tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8.compile.lock}"
23	LOG_TAG="${LOG_TAG:-tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8}"
24	NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY="${NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY:-0}"
25	KERNEL_LOG_PID=""
26	SERVER_PID=""
27	READINESS_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/v1/models"
28	
29	while (( $# > 0 )); do
30	    case "$1" in
31	        --compile-only) NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY=1 ;;
32	        *) echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
33	    esac
34	    shift
35	done
36	
37	if [[ -z "$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" ]]; then
38	    printf -v OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG \
39	        '{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":%s,"max_context_length":%s,"context_encoding_buckets":[%s],"prefix_buckets":[%s],"token_generation_buckets":[%s],"save_sharded_checkpoint":true,"blockwise_matmul_config":{"use_shard_on_intermediate_dynamic_while":true}}' \
40	        "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS" \
41	        "$COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS" "$COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS"
42	fi
43	
44	quarantine_failed_compiler_cache() {
45	    local cache_root="${NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL#file://}"
46	    local quarantine_root=""
47	    local module_dir cache_namespace lock_file first_log_line lock_fd
48	
49	    [[ -d "$cache_root" ]] || return 0
50	
51	    while IFS= read -r -d '' module_dir; do
52	        [[ -f "$module_dir/model.neff" ]] && continue
53	
54	        lock_file="$module_dir/model.hlo_module.pb.lock"
55	        lock_fd=""
56	        if [[ -f "$lock_file" ]]; then
57	            exec {lock_fd}>"$lock_file"
58	            if ! flock -n "$lock_fd"; then
59	                exec {lock_fd}>&-
60	                continue
61	            fi
62	        fi
63	
64	        first_log_line=""
65	        if [[ -f "$module_dir/model.log" ]]; then
66	            IFS= read -r first_log_line < "$module_dir/model.log" || true
67	        fi
68	        if [[ ! -f "$lock_file" && "$first_log_line" != "Failed compilation"* ]]; then
69	            continue
70	        fi
71	
72	        if [[ -z "$quarantine_root" ]]; then
73	            quarantine_root="$cache_root.failed-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
74	        fi
75	        cache_namespace="$(basename -- "$(dirname -- "$module_dir")")"
76	        mkdir -p "$quarantine_root/$cache_namespace"
77	        mv -- "$module_dir" "$quarantine_root/$cache_namespace/"
78	        echo "Quarantined failed compiler cache: $module_dir"
79	
80	        if [[ -n "$lock_fd" ]]; then
81	            exec {lock_fd}>&-
82	        fi
83	    done < <(
84	        find "$cache_root" -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d \
85	            -name 'MODULE_*' -print0
86	    )
87	
88	    if [[ -n "$quarantine_root" ]]; then
89	        echo "Failed compiler caches moved to: $quarantine_root"
90	    fi
91	}
92	
93	cleanup() {
94	    if [[ -n "$SERVER_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
95	        kill "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
96	        wait "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
97	    fi
98	    if [[ -n "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
99	        kill "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
100	        wait "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
101	    fi
102	}
103	trap cleanup EXIT
104	trap 'exit 130' INT TERM
105	
106	if [[ ! "$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]; then
107	    echo "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer." >&2
108	    exit 2
109	fi
110	if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" != "0" && "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" != "1" ]]; then
111	    echo "NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY must be 0 or 1." >&2
112	    exit 2
113	fi
114	
115	if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" && "${NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
116	    if ! command -v systemd-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
117	        echo "systemd-run is required for the Neuron compile memory limit." >&2
118	        echo "Set NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE=0 only if another cgroup limit is active." >&2
119	        exit 1
120	    fi
121	    exec systemd-run --user --scope --quiet \
122	        -p "MemoryHigh=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
123	        -p "MemoryMax=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
124	        -p MemorySwapMax=0 \
125	        env \
126	        NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE=1 \
127	        NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
128	        TP_SIZE="$TP_SIZE" \
129	        COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS="$COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS" \
130	        COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS="$COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS" \
131	        COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS="$COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS" \
132	        MAX_MODEL_LEN="$MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
133	        MAX_NUM_SEQS="$MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
134	        NEURON_SCHEDULER="${NEURON_SCHEDULER:-custom}" \
135	        ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL="${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-}" \
136	        MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS="${MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS:-}" \
137	        EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
138	        XLA_HANDLE_SPECIAL_SCALAR="${XLA_HANDLE_SPECIAL_SCALAR:-}" \
139	        UNSAFE_FP8FNCAST="${UNSAFE_FP8FNCAST:-}" \
140	        NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG="$NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG" \
141	        NEURON_CACHE_TAG="${NEURON_CACHE_TAG:-default}" \
142	        OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" \
143	        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
144	        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
145	        NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE="$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" \
146	        BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR="$BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR" \
147	        NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL="$NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL" \
148	        NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES" \
149	        COMPILE_LOCK_FILE="$COMPILE_LOCK_FILE" \
150	        LOG_TAG="$LOG_TAG" \
151	        NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY="$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" \
152	        PORT="$PORT" \
153	        bash "$0"
154	fi
155	
156	LOG_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/logs/neuron-debug/$LOG_TAG-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
157	mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
158	exec > >(tee -a "$LOG_DIR/compile.log") 2>&1
159	journalctl -kf -o short-iso > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.log" 2>&1 &
160	KERNEL_LOG_PID=$!
161	printf '%s\n' "$$" > "$LOG_DIR/compile.pid"
162	printf '%s\n' "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.pid"
163	COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER="$LOG_DIR/compile-only.complete"
164	echo "Neuron compile logs: $LOG_DIR"
165	
166	mkdir -p \
167	    "$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts" \
168	    "$BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR" \
169	    "${NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL#file://}"
170	exec 9>"$COMPILE_LOCK_FILE"
171	if ! flock -n 9; then
172	    echo "Another Tongyi precompilation is already running." >&2
173	    exit 1
174	fi
175	
176	quarantine_failed_compiler_cache
177	
178	if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" != "1" ]] && curl -fsS "$READINESS_URL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
179	    echo "A vLLM server is already responding at $READINESS_URL." >&2
180	    echo "Stop it or set PORT to an unused port before precompiling." >&2
181	    exit 1
182	fi
183	
184	echo "Precompiling Tongyi: TP=$TP_SIZE max_len=$MAX_MODEL_LEN max_seqs=$MAX_NUM_SEQS"
185	echo "Neuron scheduler: ${NEURON_SCHEDULER:-custom} (chunked_prefill=${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-default}, max_batched_tokens=${MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS:-default})"
186	echo "Compile buckets: context=[$COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS] prefix=[$COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS] generation=[$COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS]"
187	echo "Visible NeuronCores: $NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES"
188	echo "Compile work directory: $BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR"
189	echo "Compiler cache: $NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL"
190	echo "NxD HLO compile workers: $NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS"
191	if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" == "1" ]]; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="${NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS:-16}"
TP_SIZE="${TP_SIZE:-8}"
MAX_MODEL_LEN="${MAX_MODEL_LEN:-128000}"
MAX_NUM_SEQS="${MAX_NUM_SEQS:-2}"
COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS="${COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS:-16384}"
COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS="${COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS:-114688}"
COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS="${COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS:-16384,32768,65536,98304,128000}"
OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="${OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG:-}"
NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG="${NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG:-2}"
NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH:-1750G}"
NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX:-1900G}"
NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE="${NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE:-1}"
BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR="${BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compile-work/tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8}"
NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL="${NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compile-cache/tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8}"
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="${NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES:-0-7}"
COMPILE_LOCK_FILE="${COMPILE_LOCK_FILE:-$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/.tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8.compile.lock}"
LOG_TAG="${LOG_TAG:-tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8}"
NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY="${NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY:-0}"
KERNEL_LOG_PID=""
SERVER_PID=""
READINESS_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/v1/models"

while (( $# > 0 )); do
    case "$1" in
        --compile-only) NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY=1 ;;
        *) echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    esac
    shift
done

if [[ -z "$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" ]]; then
    printf -v OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG \
        '{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":%s,"max_context_length":%s,"context_encoding_buckets":[%s],"prefix_buckets":[%s],"token_generation_buckets":[%s],"save_sharded_checkpoint":true,"blockwise_matmul_config":{"use_shard_on_intermediate_dynamic_while":true}}' \
        "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS" \
        "$COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS" "$COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS"
fi

quarantine_failed_compiler_cache() {
    local cache_root="${NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL#file://}"
    local quarantine_root=""
    local module_dir cache_namespace lock_file first_log_line lock_fd

    [[ -d "$cache_root" ]] || return 0

    while IFS= read -r -d '' module_dir; do
        [[ -f "$module_dir/model.neff" ]] && continue

        lock_file="$module_dir/model.hlo_module.pb.lock"
        lock_fd=""
        if [[ -f "$lock_file" ]]; then
            exec {lock_fd}>"$lock_file"
            if ! flock -n "$lock_fd"; then
                exec {lock_fd}>&-
                continue
            fi
        fi

        first_log_line=""
        if [[ -f "$module_dir/model.log" ]]; then
            IFS= read -r first_log_line < "$module_dir/model.log" || true
        fi
        if [[ ! -f "$lock_file" && "$first_log_line" != "Failed compilation"* ]]; then
            continue
        fi

        if [[ -z "$quarantine_root" ]]; then
            quarantine_root="$cache_root.failed-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
        fi
        cache_namespace="$(basename -- "$(dirname -- "$module_dir")")"
        mkdir -p "$quarantine_root/$cache_namespace"
        mv -- "$module_dir" "$quarantine_root/$cache_namespace/"
        echo "Quarantined failed compiler cache: $module_dir"

        if [[ -n "$lock_fd" ]]; then
            exec {lock_fd}>&-
        fi
    done < <(
        find "$cache_root" -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d \
            -name 'MODULE_*' -print0
    )

    if [[ -n "$quarantine_root" ]]; then
        echo "Failed compiler caches moved to: $quarantine_root"
    fi
}

cleanup() {
    if [[ -n "$SERVER_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        kill "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        wait "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
    if [[ -n "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        kill "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        wait "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT
trap 'exit 130' INT TERM

if [[ ! "$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]; then
    echo "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer." >&2
    exit 2
fi
if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" != "0" && "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" != "1" ]]; then
    echo "NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY must be 0 or 1." >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" && "${NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
    if ! command -v systemd-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "systemd-run is required for the Neuron compile memory limit." >&2
        echo "Set NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE=0 only if another cgroup limit is active." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    exec systemd-run --user --scope --quiet \
        -p "MemoryHigh=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
        -p "MemoryMax=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
        -p MemorySwapMax=0 \
        env \
        NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE=1 \
        NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
        TP_SIZE="$TP_SIZE" \
        COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS="$COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS" \
        COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS="$COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS" \
        COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS="$COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS" \
        MAX_MODEL_LEN="$MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
        MAX_NUM_SEQS="$MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
        NEURON_SCHEDULER="${NEURON_SCHEDULER:-custom}" \
        ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL="${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-}" \
        MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS="${MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS:-}" \
        EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
        XLA_HANDLE_SPECIAL_SCALAR="${XLA_HANDLE_SPECIAL_SCALAR:-}" \
        UNSAFE_FP8FNCAST="${UNSAFE_FP8FNCAST:-}" \
        NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG="$NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG" \
        NEURON_CACHE_TAG="${NEURON_CACHE_TAG:-default}" \
        OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" \
        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
        NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE="$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" \
        BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR="$BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR" \
        NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL="$NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL" \
        NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES" \
        COMPILE_LOCK_FILE="$COMPILE_LOCK_FILE" \
        LOG_TAG="$LOG_TAG" \
        NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY="$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" \
        PORT="$PORT" \
        bash "$0"
fi

LOG_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/logs/neuron-debug/$LOG_TAG-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
exec > >(tee -a "$LOG_DIR/compile.log") 2>&1
journalctl -kf -o short-iso > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.log" 2>&1 &
KERNEL_LOG_PID=$!
printf '%s\n' "$$" > "$LOG_DIR/compile.pid"
printf '%s\n' "$KERNEL_LOG_PID" > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.pid"
COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER="$LOG_DIR/compile-only.complete"
echo "Neuron compile logs: $LOG_DIR"

mkdir -p \
    "$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts" \
    "$BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR" \
    "${NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL#file://}"
exec 9>"$COMPILE_LOCK_FILE"
if ! flock -n 9; then
    echo "Another Tongyi precompilation is already running." >&2
    exit 1
fi

quarantine_failed_compiler_cache

if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" != "1" ]] && curl -fsS "$READINESS_URL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "A vLLM server is already responding at $READINESS_URL." >&2
    echo "Stop it or set PORT to an unused port before precompiling." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Precompiling Tongyi: TP=$TP_SIZE max_len=$MAX_MODEL_LEN max_seqs=$MAX_NUM_SEQS"
echo "Neuron scheduler: ${NEURON_SCHEDULER:-custom} (chunked_prefill=${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-default}, max_batched_tokens=${MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS:-default})"
echo "Compile buckets: context=[$COMPILE_CONTEXT_BUCKETS] prefix=[$COMPILE_PREFIX_BUCKETS] generation=[$COMPILE_GENERATION_BUCKETS]"
echo "Visible NeuronCores: $NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES"
echo "Compile work directory: $BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR"
echo "Compiler cache: $NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL"
echo "NxD HLO compile workers: $NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS"
if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "Compile-only mode: skip Neuron device load, warmup, and HTTP readiness."
fi
if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "Compile memory limits: high=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH max=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX"
fi
if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "The compiler EngineCore will exit after artifact generation succeeds."
else
    echo "The temporary server will stop after compilation and load validation succeed."
fi

HOST=127.0.0.1 \
PORT="$PORT" \
BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR="$BASE_COMPILE_WORK_DIR" \
NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL="$NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL" \
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES" \
NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG="$NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG" \
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY="$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" \
NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER="$COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER" \
TP_SIZE="$TP_SIZE" \
MAX_MODEL_LEN="$MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
MAX_NUM_SEQS="$MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" \
NEURON_QUARANTINE_INCOMPLETE_ARTIFACTS=1 \
    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" &
SERVER_PID=$!

while true; do
    if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" == "1" ]]; then
        [[ -f "$COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER" ]] && break
    elif curl -fsS "$READINESS_URL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        break
    fi
    if ! kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        set +e
        wait "$SERVER_PID"
        status=$?
        set -e
        SERVER_PID=""
        if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" == "1" && -f "$COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER" ]]; then
            break
        fi
        if [[ "$status" -eq 0 ]]; then
            status=1
        fi
        echo "Tongyi precompilation failed before the server became ready." >&2
        exit "$status"
    fi
    sleep 10
done

if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "Compilation artifacts generated successfully; Neuron device load skipped."
else
    echo "Compilation artifacts loaded successfully; stopping the temporary server."
fi
cleanup
SERVER_PID=""

artifact_path="$(sed -n 's/^Neuron compiled artifacts: \([^ ]*\) .*/\1/p' "$LOG_DIR/compile.log" | tail -n 1)"
if [[ -n "$artifact_path" && -f "$artifact_path/model.pt" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$artifact_path" > "$LOG_DIR/compiled_artifact_path"
    echo "Compiled artifact path: $artifact_path"
    echo "Artifact path file: $LOG_DIR/compiled_artifact_path"
else
    echo "WARNING: compilation completed but no complete model.pt artifact path was found in $LOG_DIR/compile.log." >&2
fi

echo "Precompilation complete. Start the server with:"
echo "  bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	export MODEL="${MODEL:-Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B}"

8	export MAX_MODEL_LEN="${MAX_MODEL_LEN:-128000}"
9	export MAX_NUM_SEQS="${MAX_NUM_SEQS:-2}"
10	export BLOCK_SIZE="${BLOCK_SIZE:-32}"
11	export NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE="${NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE:-$(( (MAX_MODEL_LEN + BLOCK_SIZE - 1) / BLOCK_SIZE * MAX_NUM_SEQS ))}"
12	export ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING="${ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING:-1}"
13	export NEURON_SCHEDULER="${NEURON_SCHEDULER:-custom}"
14	export NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG="${NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG:-2}"
15	export PYTHON_BIN="${PYTHON_BIN:-$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")/../.." && pwd)/.venv-neuron/bin/python}"
16	
17	case "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" in
18	    custom)
19	        export DISABLE_NEURON_CUSTOM_SCHEDULER=0
20	        export ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL="${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-0}"
21	        echo "WARNING: custom Neuron scheduling does not chunk prefill; a cold uncached suffix larger than the compiled context bucket can terminate EngineCore." >&2
22	        ;;
23	    native)
24	        # Native vLLM scheduling is the only Neuron path that retains chunked
25	        # prefill. Validate it with a separate compiled artifact/cache first.
26	        export DISABLE_NEURON_CUSTOM_SCHEDULER=1
27	        export ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL="${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-1}"
28	        max_num_batched_tokens="${MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS:-16384}"
29	        if [[ ! "$max_num_batched_tokens" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]] \
30	            || (( max_num_batched_tokens > MAX_MODEL_LEN )); then
31	            echo "MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS must be in 1..MAX_MODEL_LEN for native scheduling." >&2
32	            exit 2
33	        fi
34	        # vLLM's native default is too small for the compiled 16k uncached
35	        # context bucket. Respect an explicit CLI override when testing a
36	        # different chunk size.
37	        if [[ " ${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-} " != *" --max-num-batched-tokens "* \
38	            && " ${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-} " != *" --max-num-batched-tokens="* ]]; then
39	            export EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="--max-num-batched-tokens $max_num_batched_tokens${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:+ $EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS}"
40	            native_batched_tokens_log="$max_num_batched_tokens"
41	        else
42	            native_batched_tokens_log="EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS override"
43	        fi
44	        ;;
45	    *)
46	        echo "NEURON_SCHEDULER must be custom or native" >&2
47	        exit 2
48	        ;;
49	esac
50	
51	export NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="${NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES:-0-7}"
52	if [[ -z "${OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG:-}" ]]; then
53	    printf -v OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG \
54	        '{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":%s,"max_context_length":%s,"context_encoding_buckets":[16384],"prefix_buckets":[114688],"token_generation_buckets":[16384,32768,65536,98304,128000],"save_sharded_checkpoint":true,"blockwise_matmul_config":{"use_shard_on_intermediate_dynamic_while":true}}' \
55	        "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN"
56	    export OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG
57	fi
58	
59	if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "native" ]]; then
60	    OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="$($PYTHON_BIN -c '
61	import json
62	import sys
63	
64	config = json.loads(sys.argv[1])
65	config.setdefault("chunked_prefill_config", {"max_num_seqs": int(sys.argv[2])})
66	print(json.dumps(config, separators=(",", ":")))
67	' "$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" "$MAX_NUM_SEQS")"
68	    export OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG
69	fi
70	
71	"$PYTHON_BIN" -c 'import json,sys; c=json.loads(sys.argv[1]); m=int(sys.argv[2]); s=c.get("context_encoding_buckets"); p=c.get("prefix_buckets"); g=c.get("token_generation_buckets"); pair=max(s)+max(p) if s and p else None; (pair is None or (pair <= 131072 and pair >= m)) or sys.exit(f"APC buckets cover {pair} tokens; require MAX_MODEL_LEN={m} without exceeding NKI limit 131072"); (g is None or (g and max(g) >= m)) or sys.exit(f"Token-generation buckets do not cover MAX_MODEL_LEN={m}")' "$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN"
72	
73	printf 'Neuron scheduler: %s (chunked_prefill=%s)\n' \
74	    "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" "$ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL"
75	if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "native" ]]; then
76	    printf 'Native max-num-batched-tokens: %s\n' \
77	        "$native_batched_tokens_log"
78	fi
79	exec "$(dirname "$0")/serve_vllm_neuron.bash"
80

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

export MODEL="${MODEL:-Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B}"
export SERVED_MODEL_NAME="${SERVED_MODEL_NAME:-$MODEL}"
export PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
export TP_SIZE="${TP_SIZE:-8}"
export MAX_MODEL_LEN="${MAX_MODEL_LEN:-128000}"
export MAX_NUM_SEQS="${MAX_NUM_SEQS:-2}"
export BLOCK_SIZE="${BLOCK_SIZE:-32}"
export NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE="${NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE:-$(( (MAX_MODEL_LEN + BLOCK_SIZE - 1) / BLOCK_SIZE * MAX_NUM_SEQS ))}"
export ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING="${ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING:-1}"
export NEURON_SCHEDULER="${NEURON_SCHEDULER:-custom}"
export NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG="${NEURON_LOGICAL_NC_CONFIG:-2}"
export PYTHON_BIN="${PYTHON_BIN:-$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")/../.." && pwd)/.venv-neuron/bin/python}"

case "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" in
    custom)
        export DISABLE_NEURON_CUSTOM_SCHEDULER=0
        export ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL="${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-0}"
        echo "WARNING: custom Neuron scheduling does not chunk prefill; a cold uncached suffix larger than the compiled context bucket can terminate EngineCore." >&2
        ;;
    native)
        # Native vLLM scheduling is the only Neuron path that retains chunked
        # prefill. Validate it with a separate compiled artifact/cache first.
        export DISABLE_NEURON_CUSTOM_SCHEDULER=1
        export ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL="${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-1}"
        max_num_batched_tokens="${MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS:-16384}"
        if [[ ! "$max_num_batched_tokens" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]] \
            || (( max_num_batched_tokens > MAX_MODEL_LEN )); then
            echo "MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS must be in 1..MAX_MODEL_LEN for native scheduling." >&2
            exit 2
        fi
        # vLLM's native default is too small for the compiled 16k uncached
        # context bucket. Respect an explicit CLI override when testing a
        # different chunk size.
        if [[ " ${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-} " != *" --max-num-batched-tokens "* \
            && " ${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-} " != *" --max-num-batched-tokens="* ]]; then
            export EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="--max-num-batched-tokens $max_num_batched_tokens${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:+ $EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS}"
            native_batched_tokens_log="$max_num_batched_tokens"
        else
            native_batched_tokens_log="EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS override"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        echo "NEURON_SCHEDULER must be custom or native" >&2
        exit 2
        ;;
esac

export NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="${NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES:-0-7}"
if [[ -z "${OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG:-}" ]]; then
    printf -v OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG \
        '{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":%s,"max_context_length":%s,"context_encoding_buckets":[16384],"prefix_buckets":[114688],"token_generation_buckets":[16384,32768,65536,98304,128000],"save_sharded_checkpoint":true,"blockwise_matmul_config":{"use_shard_on_intermediate_dynamic_while":true}}' \
        "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN"
    export OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG
fi

if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "native" ]]; then
    OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="$($PYTHON_BIN -c '
import json
import sys

config = json.loads(sys.argv[1])
config.setdefault("chunked_prefill_config", {"max_num_seqs": int(sys.argv[2])})
print(json.dumps(config, separators=(",", ":")))
' "$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" "$MAX_NUM_SEQS")"
    export OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG
fi

"$PYTHON_BIN" -c 'import json,sys; c=json.loads(sys.argv[1]); m=int(sys.argv[2]); s=c.get("context_encoding_buckets"); p=c.get("prefix_buckets"); g=c.get("token_generation_buckets"); pair=max(s)+max(p) if s and p else None; (pair is None or (pair <= 131072 and pair >= m)) or sys.exit(f"APC buckets cover {pair} tokens; require MAX_MODEL_LEN={m} without exceeding NKI limit 131072"); (g is None or (g and max(g) >= m)) or sys.exit(f"Token-generation buckets do not cover MAX_MODEL_LEN={m}")' "$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN"

printf 'Neuron scheduler: %s (chunked_prefill=%s)\n' \
    "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" "$ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL"
if [[ "$NEURON_SCHEDULER" == "native" ]]; then
    printf 'Native max-num-batched-tokens: %s\n' \
        "$native_batched_tokens_log"
fi
exec "$(dirname "$0")/serve_vllm_neuron.bash"

1	"""Launch vLLM with a bounded NxD HLO compilation thread pool."""
2	
3	import concurrent.futures
4	import json
5	import os
6	import runpy
7	from pathlib import Path
8	
9	
10	DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS = 8
11	_original_engine_core_entrypoint = None
12	_original_kv_quantization_config_init = None
13	
14	
15	def get_compile_max_workers() -> int:
16	    raw_value = os.environ.get(
17	        "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", str(DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS)
18	    )
19	    try:
20	        max_workers = int(raw_value)
21	    except ValueError as exc:
22	        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer") from exc
23	    if max_workers < 1:
24	        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer")
25	    return max_workers
26	
27	
28	class _FuturesProxy:
29	    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
30	        self.default_max_workers = default_max_workers
31	
32	    def ThreadPoolExecutor(self, max_workers=None, *args, **kwargs):
33	        if max_workers is None:
34	            max_workers = self.default_max_workers
35	        return concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
36	            max_workers=max_workers, *args, **kwargs
37	        )
38	
39	    def __getattr__(self, name):
40	        return getattr(concurrent.futures, name)
41	
42	
43	class _ConcurrentProxy:
44	    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
45	        self.futures = _FuturesProxy(default_max_workers)
46	
47	
48	def limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers: int) -> None:
49	    from neuronx_distributed.trace import model_builder
50	
51	    # ModelBuilder creates an unbounded-by-workload executor. Replace only its
52	    # module reference so other vLLM thread pools keep their original defaults.
53	    model_builder.concurrent = _ConcurrentProxy(max_workers)
54	
55	
56	def normalize_kv_quant_dtype(kv_quant_config: dict) -> None:
57	    quant_dtype = kv_quant_config.get("quant_dtype")
58	    if isinstance(quant_dtype, str):
59	        import torch
60	
61	        try:
62	            dtype = getattr(torch, quant_dtype)
63	        except AttributeError as exc:
64	            raise ValueError(
65	                f"Unsupported KV-cache quantization dtype: {quant_dtype!r}"
66	            ) from exc
67	        if not isinstance(dtype, torch.dtype):
68	            raise ValueError(
69	                f"Unsupported KV-cache quantization dtype: {quant_dtype!r}"
70	            )
71	        kv_quant_config["quant_dtype"] = dtype
72	
73	    serialized_methods = [
74	        name
75	        for name in ("k_quant_method", "v_quant_method")
76	        if isinstance(kv_quant_config.get(name), dict)
77	    ]
78	    if not serialized_methods:
79	        return
80	
81	    from neuronx_distributed.quantization.quantization_config import (
82	        QuantizationType,
83	    )
84	
85	    for name in serialized_methods:
86	        kv_quant_config[name] = QuantizationType(kv_quant_config[name]["_value_"])
87	
88	
89	def _kv_quantization_config_init_with_torch_dtype(self, **kwargs):
90	    normalize_kv_quant_dtype(kwargs)
91	    return _original_kv_quantization_config_init(self, **kwargs)
92	
93	
94	def install_kv_quant_dtype_hook() -> None:
95	    global _original_kv_quantization_config_init
96	
97	    from neuronx_distributed.quantization.quantization_config import (
98	        KVQuantizationConfig,
99	    )
100	
101	    if (
102	        KVQuantizationConfig.__init__
103	        is _kv_quantization_config_init_with_torch_dtype
104	    ):
105	        return
106	    _original_kv_quantization_config_init = KVQuantizationConfig.__init__
107	    KVQuantizationConfig.__init__ = _kv_quantization_config_init_with_torch_dtype
108	
109	
110	def _signal_compile_only_complete(compiled_path: str) -> None:
111	    marker_value = os.environ.get("NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER")
112	    if not marker_value:
113	        raise RuntimeError(
114	            "NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY_MARKER must be set when NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY=1"
115	        )
116	    marker = Path(marker_value)
117	    marker.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
118	    marker.write_text(f"{compiled_path}\n")
119	    raise SystemExit(0)
120	
121	
122	def _compiled_artifact_is_complete(compiled_path: str) -> bool:
123	    artifact_dir = Path(compiled_path)
124	    config_path = artifact_dir / "neuron_config.json"
125	    if not (artifact_dir / "model.pt").is_file() or not config_path.is_file():
126	        return False
127	
128	    config = json.loads(config_path.read_text()).get("neuron_config", {})
129	    if not config.get("save_sharded_checkpoint", False):
130	        return True
131	
132	    world_size = int(config.get("world_size", 0))
133	    return world_size > 0 and all(
134	        (
135	            artifact_dir
136	            / "weights"
137	            / f"tp{rank}_sharded_checkpoint.safetensors"
138	        ).is_file()
139	        for rank in range(world_size)
140	    )
141	
142	
143	def _compile_model_without_loading(
144	    application, model_path, neuronx_model_cls, config, compiled_path
145	):
146	    application.model = neuronx_model_cls(model_path, config)
147	    if config.neuron_config.quantized:
148	        neuronx_model_cls.save_quantized_state_dict(model_path, config)
149	    application.model.compile(compiled_path)
150	    _signal_compile_only_complete(compiled_path)
151	
152	
153	def _reuse_compiled_model_without_loading(
154	    application, compiled_path, neuronx_model_cls, kwargs
155	):
156	    if not _compiled_artifact_is_complete(compiled_path):
157	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Incomplete compiled artifacts at {compiled_path}")
158	    _signal_compile_only_complete(compiled_path)
159	
160	
161	def install_compile_only_hook() -> None:
162	    if os.environ.get("NEURON_COMPILE_ONLY", "0") != "1":
163	        return
164	
165	    from vllm_neuron.worker.neuronx_distributed_model_loader import NeuronModelBase
166	
167	    NeuronModelBase._compile_and_load_model = _compile_model_without_loading
168	    NeuronModelBase._load_compiled_model = _reuse_compiled_model_without_loading
169	
170	
171	def _run_with_limited_nxd_workers(target, *args, **kwargs):
172	    max_workers = get_compile_max_workers()
173	    limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers)
174	    install_kv_quant_dtype_hook()
175	    install_compile_only_hook()
176	    print(f"NxD HLO compile workers in EngineCore: {max_workers}", flush=True)
177	    return target(*args, **kwargs)
178	
179	
180	def _run_engine_core_with_limited_nxd_workers(*args, **kwargs):
181	    target = _original_engine_core_entrypoint
182	    if target is None:
183	        from vllm.v1.engine.core import EngineCoreProc
184	
185	        target = EngineCoreProc.run_engine_core
186	    return _run_with_limited_nxd_workers(target, *args, **kwargs)
187	
188	
189	def install_engine_core_worker_hook() -> None:
190	    global _original_engine_core_entrypoint
191	
192	    from vllm.v1.engine.core import EngineCoreProc
193	
194	    if EngineCoreProc.run_engine_core is _run_engine_core_with_limited_nxd_workers:
195	        return
196	    _original_engine_core_entrypoint = EngineCoreProc.run_engine_core
197	    EngineCoreProc.run_engine_core = staticmethod(
198	        _run_engine_core_with_limited_nxd_workers
199	    )
200	
201	
202	def main() -> None:
203	    max_workers = get_compile_max_workers()
204	    limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers)
205	    install_engine_core_worker_hook()
206	    print(f"NxD HLO compile workers: {max_workers}", flush=True)
207	    runpy.run_module("vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server", run_name="__main__")
208	
209	
210	if __name__ == "__main__":
211	    main()
212

Now I understand the current setup. Let me start the grilling session. **First question:** You want to migrate to Docker-based vLLM-Neuron. Looking at the new setup guide, there are three deployment options: source install, AMI, or Docker container. Which **specific Docker image** do you want to use — the official AWS Neuron Deep Learning Container (DLC) mentioned in the guide, or do you want to build a custom image based on it?

**My recommendation:** Use the official AWS Neuron DLC directly (`public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest` or a pinned version tag). Building custom requires maintaining Dockerfile + dependencies, while the DLC is pre-validated. Only build custom if you need packages the DLC can't accommodate.

content.text:
好的，已经有官方DLC镜像了。

**第二个问题：** 在当前代码中，你同时运行多个Neuron服务：
- 3个TP16 Tongyi规划副本 (ports 6008-6010, cores 0-47)
- 1个TP8 embedding服务 (port 6030, cores 56-63)
- 可选的TP8 sidecar (port 6011, cores 48-55)
- 16个TP4 judge副本 (ports 8032-8047, 全部64核心用于评估阶段)

你想要的Docker部署拓扑是什么？

**选项A：** 每个服务一个独立容器 (如 `tongyi-6008`, `tongyi-6009`, `tongyi-6010`, `embedding-6030`)，每个容器映射特定的NeuronCore范围？

**选项B：** 一个长期运行的容器，在容器内通过bash脚本启动/停止不同服务（类似现在的 `n_tongyi.bash` 编排方式）？

**选项C：** 混合方式 - 规划服务用独立容器，但judge集群临时启动在一个容器内？

**我的建议：** 选项A（每服务一容器）。原因：
- 隔离更清晰，每个容器只绑定它需要的NeuronCores (`NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES`)
- 可以独立重启一个崩溃的副本而不影响其他服务
- 符合微服务模式，与你现有的多端点客户端架构对齐
- 缺点是要管理更多容器，但`docker-compose`或简单的bash循环可以解决

你倾向哪一个？

content.text:
好，一个容器内运行所有服务。

**第三个问题：** 编译产物和模型权重的存储策略。

当前设置把所有编译产物和模型都放在宿主机的 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/`：
- `local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/<hash>/` - 编译好的Neuron模型
- `local-models/neuron-compile-cache/` - Neuron编译器缓存
- `local-models/neuron-compile-work/` - 编译工作目录
- `local-models/<model-id>/` - HuggingFace模型权重

这些目录总共可能有**几百GB**。在Docker化后，你想怎么处理这些数据？

**选项A：** Docker volume挂载整个 `local-models/` 到容器内的相同路径 (`/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models`)，继续使用宿主机存储？

**选项B：** 容器内独立存储（如 `/workspace/models/`），与宿主机完全隔离，编译产物只在容器内部？

**选项C：** 分层挂载 - 只读挂载已编译的artifacts（因为已经编译好了），可写挂载编译缓存和工作目录？

**我的建议：** 选项A。原因：
- 你已经有编译好的artifacts在 `local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/6367e6277bb...` 等目录
- 重新编译会花费大量时间和内存
- 容器重启/重建时不会丢失编译产物
- 宿主机和容器可以共享相同的编译缓存，避免重复工作

但这要求容器内的路径结构与宿主机一致。你接受这个约束吗，还是想要不同的映射方式？

content.text:
好的，挂载整个项目目录到容器内相同路径。

**第四个问题：** 关于你 `~/.bashrc` 中的模型缓存路径设置。

我需要先看看你设置了什么。容器内的环境变量配置会影响：
- HuggingFace模型下载位置 (`HF_HOME`, `HUGGINGFACE_HUB_CACHE`, `TRANSFORMERS_CACHE`)
- vLLM缓存位置 (`VLLM_CACHE_ROOT` - 新版vLLM-Neuron用这个)
- Neuron编译缓存 (`NEURON_COMPILE_CACHE_URL`)

能否告诉我你在 `~/.bashrc` 中设置的是哪些环境变量和路径？比如：

```bash
grep -E "(HF_HOME|HUGGINGFACE|TRANSFORMERS_CACHE|VLLM_CACHE)" ~/.bashrc
```

**潜在影响：**
- 如果你在宿主机 `~/.bashrc` 设置了 `HF_HOME=/some/path`，容器内**不会自动继承**这个设置（容器有自己的环境）
- 如果路径指向 `/dev3` 之外的位置，需要额外的volume挂载
- 如果路径在 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus` 内部，应该没问题（已经挂载了）

你能先告诉我具体设置了什么吗？然后我们决定是否需要在 `docker run` 时通过 `-e` 传递这些环境变量。

[ -z "$PS1" ] && source /etc/profile.d/dlami.sh
# ~/.bashrc: executed by bash(1) for non-login shells.
# see /usr/share/doc/bash/examples/startup-files (in the package bash-doc)
# for examples

# If not running interactively, don't do anything
case $- in
    *i*) ;;
      *) return;;
esac

# don't put duplicate lines or lines starting with space in the history.
# See bash(1) for more options
HISTCONTROL=ignoreboth

# append to the history file, don't overwrite it
shopt -s histappend

# for setting history length see HISTSIZE and HISTFILESIZE in bash(1)
HISTSIZE=1000
HISTFILESIZE=2000

# check the window size after each command and, if necessary,
# update the values of LINES and COLUMNS.
shopt -s checkwinsize

# If set, the pattern "**" used in a pathname expansion context will
# match all files and zero or more directories and subdirectories.
#shopt -s globstar

# make less more friendly for non-text input files, see lesspipe(1)
[ -x /usr/bin/lesspipe ] && eval "$(SHELL=/bin/sh lesspipe)"

# set variable identifying the chroot you work in (used in the prompt below)
if [ -z "${debian_chroot:-}" ] && [ -r /etc/debian_chroot ]; then
    debian_chroot=$(cat /etc/debian_chroot)
fi

# set a fancy prompt (non-color, unless we know we "want" color)
case "$TERM" in
    xterm-color|*-256color) color_prompt=yes;;
esac

# uncomment for a colored prompt, if the terminal has the capability; turned
# off by default to not distract the user: the focus in a terminal window
# should be on the output of commands, not on the prompt
#force_color_prompt=yes

if [ -n "$force_color_prompt" ]; then
    if [ -x /usr/bin/tput ] && tput setaf 1 >&/dev/null; then
	# We have color support; assume it's compliant with Ecma-48
	# (ISO/IEC-6429). (Lack of such support is extremely rare, and such
	# a case would tend to support setf rather than setaf.)
	color_prompt=yes
    else
	color_prompt=
    fi
fi

if [ "$color_prompt" = yes ]; then
    PS1='${debian_chroot:+($debian_chroot)}\[\033[01;32m\]\u@\h\[\033[00m\]:\[\033[01;34m\]\w\[\033[00m\]\$ '
else
    PS1='${debian_chroot:+($debian_chroot)}\u@\h:\w\$ '
fi
unset color_prompt force_color_prompt

# If this is an xterm set the title to user@host:dir
case "$TERM" in
xterm*|rxvt*)
    PS1="\[\e]0;${debian_chroot:+($debian_chroot)}\u@\h: \w\a\]$PS1"
    ;;
*)
    ;;
esac

# enable color support of ls and also add handy aliases
if [ -x /usr/bin/dircolors ]; then
    test -r ~/.dircolors && eval "$(dircolors -b ~/.dircolors)" || eval "$(dircolors -b)"
    alias ls='ls --color=auto'
    #alias dir='dir --color=auto'
    #alias vdir='vdir --color=auto'

    alias grep='grep --color=auto'
    alias fgrep='fgrep --color=auto'
    alias egrep='egrep --color=auto'
fi

# colored GCC warnings and errors
#export GCC_COLORS='error=01;31:warning=01;35:note=01;36:caret=01;32:locus=01:quote=01'

# some more ls aliases
alias ll='ls -alF'
alias la='ls -A'
alias l='ls -CF'

# Add an "alert" alias for long running commands.  Use like so:
#   sleep 10; alert
alias alert='notify-send --urgency=low -i "$([ $? = 0 ] && echo terminal || echo error)" "$(history|tail -n1|sed -e '\''s/^\s*[0-9]\+\s*//;s/[;&|]\s*alert$//'\'')"'

# Alias definitions.
# You may want to put all your additions into a separate file like
# ~/.bash_aliases, instead of adding them here directly.
# See /usr/share/doc/bash-doc/examples in the bash-doc package.

if [ -f ~/.bash_aliases ]; then
    . ~/.bash_aliases
fi

# enable programmable completion features (you don't need to enable
# this, if it's already enabled in /etc/bash.bashrc and /etc/profile
# sources /etc/bash.bashrc).
if ! shopt -oq posix; then
  if [ -f /usr/share/bash-completion/bash_completion ]; then
    . /usr/share/bash-completion/bash_completion
  elif [ -f /etc/bash_completion ]; then
    . /etc/bash_completion
  fi
fi

. "$HOME/.local/bin/env"
export MICU_API_KEY='[REDACTED_OPENAI_KEY]'
export CODEX_HOME=/dev3/codex-home
export CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=/dev3/cc
export AWS_KEY=[REDACTED_AWS_BEARER_TOKEN]

content.text:
看到了。你的 `.bashrc` 设置了：
- `VLLM_CACHE_ROOT=/dev3/zigeng/vLLM_cache` (line 148)
- `HF_TOKEN` (line 149，但格式有问题，看起来有两个token)

**第四个问题（续）：** 这些路径的处理方式。

`VLLM_CACHE_ROOT=/dev3/zigeng/vLLM_cache` 指向 `/dev3` 下的目录，但**不在**你的项目目录 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus` 内。

**问题是：** 如果只挂载 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus` 到容器，容器内访问 `/dev3/zigeng/vLLM_cache` 会失败（不存在）。

**选项A：** 在容器启动时通过 `-v` 额外挂载这个缓存目录：
```bash
docker run -v /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus:/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus \
           -v /dev3/zigeng/vLLM_cache:/dev3/zigeng/vLLM_cache \
           ...
```

**选项B：** 在容器内覆盖 `VLLM_CACHE_ROOT`，指向项目内部的某个位置，比如：
```bash
docker run -e VLLM_CACHE_ROOT=/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache \
           ...
```
这样所有缓存都在一个挂载点下，但会改变现有的vLLM缓存位置。

**选项C：** 更激进的挂载 - 直接挂载整个 `/dev3` 到容器：
```bash
docker run -v /dev3:/dev3 ...
```
这样所有 `/dev3` 下的路径在容器内都可见。

**我的建议：** 选项C（挂载整个 `/dev3`）。原因：
- 你的数据都在 `/dev3` 下（项目、缓存、codex-home等）
- 避免管理多个volume挂载点
- 容器内路径与宿主机完全一致，不会有路径错乱
- 缺点是容器能看到 `/dev3` 下的所有内容，但对于单用户开发环境这不是问题

你接受这个方案吗？

message.content: 修改.bashrc中的内容到/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache中

content.thinking:
用户要求修改 `.bashrc` 中的 `VLLM_CACHE_ROOT` 路径，从 `/dev3/zigeng/vLLM_cache` 改为 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache`。

这样做的好处是所有缓存都在项目目录内，只需要挂载一个目录 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus` 就可以了。

我应该直接修改这个文件。同时注意到 line 149 的 HF_TOKEN 格式有问题，有两个token值，我应该一起修复。

content.text: 好的，修改 `.bashrc` 把缓存路径移到项目内部。

content.text:
已修改。现在继续提问。

**第五个问题：** Python环境的选择。

当前你的项目使用 `uv` 管理Python环境，有两个虚拟环境：
- `.venv` - 主环境（CPU客户端、评估、检索）
- `.venv-neuron` - Neuron环境（你的脚本中 `PYTHON_BIN` 默认指向这个）

Docker容器内运行时，你想用哪种Python环境策略？

**选项A：** 使用DLC自带的Python环境（已预装vLLM-Neuron和Neuron SDK），**不使用**你宿主机的 `.venv` 或 `.venv-neuron`？

**选项B：** 挂载项目后，在容器内**激活宿主机的** `.venv-neuron`，继续使用宿主机编译的Python包？

**选项C：** 容器内重新创建一个Neuron虚拟环境，独立于宿主机环境？

**我的建议：** 选项A（使用DLC自带环境）。原因：
- DLC的Python环境已经针对Neuron优化和测试过
- 避免宿主机虚拟环境与容器内系统库的ABI冲突
- 新版vLLM-Neuron可能依赖不同版本的包，重新开始更干净
- 你的 `serve_tongyi.bash` 中 `PYTHON_BIN` 默认指向 `.venv-neuron/bin/python`，在容器内需要改成 DLC 的 Python（如 `/opt/conda/bin/python` 或 `python3`）

这意味着你需要在容器内安装你项目的依赖（如果需要的话）。你的项目代码需要被导入吗（`search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py`），还是只运行vLLM服务器？

message.content: 使用A。项目代码也要在容器中，也就是说我的所有运行内容都放在容器中，包括client server retriever

content.thinking:
好的，用户选择选项A - 使用DLC自带的Python环境。

而且用户明确说：**所有运行内容都放在容器中，包括client server retriever**。

这是一个重要的澄清。这意味着：
1. 不只是vLLM服务器在容器内
2. 整个工作流都在容器内运行：
   - Neuron vLLM服务器（Tongyi规划模型、embedding、judge）
   - 客户端代码（`search_agent/tongyi_client.py`等）
   - 检索器（FAISS、BM25等）
   - 评估脚本（`scripts_evaluation/evaluate_run.py`）

这带来新的考虑点：

**Python依赖管理：**
- 用户项目有 `pyproject.toml` 和 `uv.lock`
- DLC可能没有用户项目需要的所有依赖（如OpenAI客户端、anthropic、FAISS、pyserini等）
- 需要在容器内安装项目依赖

**当前的问题是：** DLC自带的环境有vLLM-Neuron，但用户的项目需要很多其他包。应该怎么安装这些依赖？

**选项：**
1. 在容器内直接 `pip install` 到DLC的系统Python
2. 在容器内用uv创建新虚拟环境（但这和"使用DLC环境"有点冲突）
3. 修改Dockerfile创建自定义镜像，预装依赖（但用户说"所有的config让我来设置"，暗示不想动Dockerfile）
4. 容器启动时运行初始化脚本安装依赖

还需要确认：DLC环境中如何最好地集成用户项目的依赖？另外，当前代码用了自定义的 `search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py` 来启动vLLM，这个launcher做了很多魔改（限制NxD编译worker、KV量化dtype hook、compile-only模式等），新的vLLM-Neuron 0.21版本可能不

需要这些hack，或者需要不同的方式。得确认是否继续使用这个launcher，还是直接用官方的 `vllm serve` 或 `python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server`。

content.text:
好的，所有运行内容都在容器内。

**第六个问题：** Python项目依赖的安装策略。

你的项目有 `pyproject.toml`，定义了很多依赖（OpenAI客户端、Anthropic、FAISS、Pyserini、pandas等）。DLC自带vLLM-Neuron和Neuron SDK，但**不会有**你项目特定的依赖。

在容器内如何安装你的项目依赖？

**选项A：** 容器启动后，手动在DLC的Python环境中安装：
```bash
# 在容器内
cd /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
pip install -e .
# 或者
uv pip install --system -e .
```
每次启动新容器都要重新安装。

**选项B：** 写一个容器启动脚本（`entrypoint.sh`），自动检查并安装依赖：
```bash
#!/bin/bash
cd /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
pip install -e . || true
exec "$@"
```
容器启动时自动安装，但每次重启都会重新检查。

**选项C：** 基于DLC构建自定义镜像，在Dockerfile中预装项目依赖：
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
COPY pyproject.toml /workspace/
RUN pip install -e /workspace
```
构建一次，以后直接用，但需要维护Dockerfile。

**选项D：** 在容器内创建持久化的虚拟环境层（如 `local-models/docker-venv/`），第一次安装后复用：
```bash
python -m venv /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/docker-venv
source /dev3/.../docker-venv/bin/activate
pip install -e /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
```
虚拟环境在挂载的目录中，容器重启后仍然存在。

**我的建议：** 选项D（持久化虚拟环境）。原因：
- 不修改DLC的系统Python（保持干净）
- 不需要维护Dockerfile（你说config自己设置，暗示不想碰Dockerfile）
- 第一次安装后，重启容器直接可用
- 可以独立于DLC环境升级项目依赖

但这会让环境变复杂一点（DLC Python + 你的venv）。你能接受吗，还是更倾向其他方案？

message.content: 基于DLC构建自定义镜像

content.thinking:
用户选择了选项C - 基于DLC构建自定义镜像，在Dockerfile中预装项目依赖。但之前用户说"所有的config让我来设置"，现在又选择要Dockerfile，这说明用户愿意接受Dockerfile这种配置方式。

这就引出了下一个关键问题：Dockerfile应该如何构建？需要考虑：
1. 是复制整个项目到镜像内部，还是只复制依赖文件（pyproject.toml）然后挂载代码？
2. 工作目录应该设置在哪里？
3. 是否需要设置环境变量（VLLM_CACHE_ROOT, HF_TOKEN等）？
4. 是否需要安装Java 21（Pyserini需要）？
5. 是否需要处理flash-attn等特殊包？

我应该继续提问，探讨Dockerfile的构建策略。

content.text:
好的，构建自定义镜像。

**第七个问题：** Dockerfile的构建策略 - 代码层的处理。

构建镜像时，项目代码如何放入镜像？

**选项A：** 只复制 `pyproject.toml` 和 `README.md` 到镜像内，安装依赖，**不复制源代码**。运行时通过volume挂载 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`：
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
WORKDIR /workspace
COPY pyproject.toml README.md ./
RUN pip install -e .
# 源代码在运行时挂载
```
好处：修改代码不需要重新构建镜像，代码更新即时生效。
坏处：镜像不完全自包含，必须挂载才能运行。

**选项B：** 复制完整项目到镜像内的某个路径（如 `/opt/browsecomp/`），安装后就自包含。运行时**仍然挂载**宿主机项目目录到 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus` 覆盖镜像内的代码：
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
COPY . /opt/browsecomp
WORKDIR /opt/browsecomp
RUN pip install -e .
# 运行时挂载会覆盖 /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
```
好处：镜像自包含，可以不挂载运行（但你要挂载数据）。
坏处：镜像会比较大，而且运行时挂载会覆盖镜像内的代码。

**选项C：** 混合 - 复制项目到 `/opt/browsecomp`，安装到系统环境，但运行时工作目录用挂载的 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`：
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
COPY . /tmp/build
RUN pip install /tmp/build && rm -rf /tmp/build
WORKDIR /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
# 包已安装到site-packages，代码通过挂载提供
```
好处：包安装在系统里（如 `search_agent` 模块可导入），代码修改仍然生效。
坏处：如果 `pyproject.toml` 用的是 `pip install -e`（可编辑模式），这种方式不行。

**我的建议：** 选项A（只复制依赖文件）。原因：
- 你说"所有开发文件"都要在挂载的目录中可见
- 代码修改不需要重建镜像，符合开发迭代流程
- 镜像更小，构建更快
- 但这意味着镜像必须和挂载一起用，不能独立分发

你接受吗？还是想要完全自包含的镜像（选项B）？

message.content: A尽量尽量只使用挂载

content.thinking:
用户选择了选项A，强调"尽量尽量只使用挂载"。这意味着镜像应该非常轻量，只包含依赖安装，所有代码、数据、配置都通过挂载提供。

现在继续下一个问题。我需要问关于Dockerfile中具体如何安装依赖的问题，特别是：
- 是否需要分层优化（先装不变的依赖，再装项目依赖）
- 如何处理 `pip install -e .` （editable模式需要源代码存在）
- 是否需要处理一些特殊的依赖（如 flash-attn，虽然Neuron环境可能不需要）

让我继续问第八个问题。

content.text:
好的，最大化使用挂载，镜像只装依赖。

**第八个问题：** Dockerfile中依赖安装的具体方式。

你的 `pyproject.toml` 定义了很多依赖，但问题是 **`pip install -e .`（editable模式）需要源代码目录存在**。在镜像构建时源代码不在，只有 `pyproject.toml`。

如何处理？

**选项A：** 构建时先复制最小代码骨架，安装为editable，然后删除代码（保留安装记录）：
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
WORKDIR /workspace
COPY pyproject.toml README.md ./
COPY search_agent/__init__.py searcher/__init__.py ./dummy/
RUN pip install -e . && rm -rf dummy/
# 运行时挂载真实代码
```
技巧性强，可能不稳定。

**选项B：** 改用非editable模式安装依赖，但不安装你的包本身：
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
WORKDIR /workspace
COPY pyproject.toml ./
RUN pip install --no-deps $(python -c "import tomllib; print(' '.join(tomllib.load(open('pyproject.toml','rb'))['project']['dependencies']))")
# 只装依赖，不装项目本身
```
手动提取依赖列表安装。运行时Python能找到模块（因为 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus` 在 `PYTHONPATH` 或 `sys.path` 中）。

**选项C：** 创建一个单独的 `requirements.txt`，只列依赖包，不包含项目本身：
```bash
# 在宿主机先生成
uv pip compile pyproject.toml -o docker/requirements.txt
```
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
COPY docker/requirements.txt /tmp/
RUN pip install -r /tmp/requirements.txt
```
最清晰，但需要维护额外文件。

**选项D：** 在容器启动脚本中安装（entrypoint），每次启动都执行：
```dockerfile
FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/vllm-inference:neuronx-latest
COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh
ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
```
```bash
#!/bin/bash
cd /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
pip install -e . --no-deps  # 只建立editable link，依赖已在镜像中
exec "$@"
```
启动慢，但灵活。

**我的建议：** 选项C（requirements.txt）。原因：
- 最标准、最可靠的方式
- 镜像构建可重现
- 依赖固定在文件中，便于审查和版本控制
- 运行时通过 `PYTHONPATH` 或在启动脚本中 `cd` 到项目目录，Python就能找到你的模块

你能接受维护一个 `docker/requirements.txt` 吗？还是更倾向其他方案？

message.content: 接受

content.thinking:
好的，用户接受维护 `docker/requirements.txt`。现在继续下一个问题。

让我想想接下来需要问什么：
1. ✅ Docker部署拓扑（选了B：一个容器内运行所有服务）
2. ✅ 编译产物和模型存储（选了A：挂载 local-models/）
3. ✅ 挂载策略（决定挂载整个项目目录）
4. ✅ .bashrc 环境变量（已修改到项目内）
5. ✅ Python环境（选了A：使用DLC自带环境）
6. ✅ 项目依赖安装（选了C：构建自定义镜像）
7. ✅ 代码层处理（选了A：只复制依赖文件，源码挂载）
8. ✅ 依赖安装方式（选了C：requirements.txt）

接下来应该问的关键决策：

9. **容器的网络模式** - 因为有多个服务在不同端口（6008-6010, 6030, 8032-8047等），需要决定网络策略
10. **NeuronCore的访问权限** - Docker需要特殊权限访问 `/dev/neuron*` 设备
11. **容器启动方式** - 如何启动容器（docker run命令的结构）
12. **旧的编译脚本和Python launcher的迁移** - 这些依赖特定路径和环境，在Docker中需要调整
13. **容器内的工作流程** - 进入容器后如何执行编译、服务启动等操作
14. **数据持久化和日志管理** - 编译日志、服务日志、模型缓存等的存储位置

现在提出第9个问题：关于容器网络配置。

content.text:
好的，接受 `requirements.txt` 方案。

**第九个问题：** Docker的网络模式选择。

你的服务监听多个端口：
- Tongyi规划: 6008-6010 (TP16×3)
- Tongyi sidecar: 6011 (TP8×1, 可选)
- Embedding: 6030 (TP8×1)
- Judge: 8032-8047 (TP4×16)

客户端脚本（`search_agent/tongyi_client.py` 等）需要连接这些端口。客户端也在容器内运行。

Docker网络模式选择：

**选项A：** `--network host` - 容器直接使用宿主机网络栈：
```bash
docker run --network host ...
```
好处：容器内的服务直接绑定到宿主机的端口，`localhost:6008` 在容器内外含义相同，不需要端口映射。
坏处：容器不再有网络隔离，所有端口直接暴露到宿主机。

**选项B：** 桥接网络 + 端口映射：
```bash
docker run -p 6008:6008 -p 6009:6009 -p 6010:6010 -p 6030:6030 -p 8032-8047:8032-8047 ...
```
好处：网络隔离，可以控制哪些端口暴露。
坏处：需要映射很多端口（特别是judge的16个端口），命令行很长。客户端如果在容器内，用 `localhost` 就行；如果在容器外，需要用宿主机IP。

**选项C：** 桥接网络，但**不映射端口**（因为客户端也在容器内）：
```bash
docker run ...  # 不加 -p
```
容器内的服务和客户端通过容器内部的 `localhost` 通信，外部无法访问。
好处：最简洁，符合"所有运行内容都在容器内"的原则。
坏处：宿主机无法直接访问容器内的服务（无法用 `curl localhost:6008` 测试），调试不便。

**我的建议：** 选项A (`--network host`)。原因：
- 你的所有服务都在一个容器内，不需要容器间网络
- 脚本中的 `localhost:6008` 等地址不需要改动
- 你可以从宿主机直接访问容器内的服务进行调试/测试
- Trn2是专用实例，没有多租户网络隔离需求
- 最简单，没有端口映射的复杂性

你接受吗？还是想要网络隔离（选项B或C）？

message.content: A

content.thinking:
好的，用户接受了选项A (`--network host`)。现在继续下一个问题。

让我看看还有什么关键决策需要确认：
1. ✅ Docker部署拓扑 - 一个容器内运行所有服务
2. ✅ 存储策略 - 挂载整个项目目录，使用项目内的 local-models/
3. ✅ 修改了 .bashrc 把缓存路径移到项目内
4. ✅ Python环境 - 使用DLC自带环境
5. ✅ 所有运行内容都在容器内
6. ✅ 构建自定义镜像
7. ✅ Dockerfile只复制依赖文件，代码通过挂载
8. ✅ 使用 requirements.txt 安装依赖
9. ✅ 网络模式用 --network host

下一步应该问：
- 容器的启动命令/entrypoint是什么？是直接bash，还是运行特定脚本？
- Neuron驱动/设备访问 - 需要 --device 或 --privileged
- 环境变量的传递（HF_TOKEN等）
- 工作目录的设置
- 容器的命名和生命周期管理
- 要不要保留旧的bash脚本，还是全部重写为Docker版本
- neuron_vllm_launcher.py 这种自定义启动器还需要吗
- 编译产物的迁移 - 旧的编译产物能在新版vLLM-Neuron中使用吗
- 设备访问和启动方式应该优先确认。

content.text:
好的，用 `--network host`。

**第十个问题：** Neuron设备访问配置。

容器需要访问Trn2的NeuronCores。Docker需要特殊配置来让容器看到Neuron设备。

AWS Neuron的Docker文档通常要求：

**选项A：** 使用 `--device` 挂载Neuron设备：
```bash
docker run --device=/dev/neuron0 --device=/dev/neuron1 ... \
           --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --cap-add IPC_LOCK \
           ...
```
需要手动指定每个设备节点。

**选项B：** 使用 `--privileged` 模式（给容器完全硬件访问权限）：
```bash
docker run --privileged ...
```
简单但不安全，容器有完全硬件访问权限。

**选项C：** 挂载 `/dev` 目录 + capabilities：
```bash
docker run -v /dev:/dev \
           --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --cap-add IPC_LOCK \
           ...
```
容器能看到所有 `/dev` 下的设备。

**选项D：** 查看官方DLC的推荐启动参数（AWS文档或镜像说明中应该有标准的 docker run 命令）。

你下载的官方DLC镜像有没有附带运行说明？通常AWS会提供标准的启动命令。如果有，能否贴一下？

如果没有，**我的建议：** 先尝试选项C（挂载 `/dev` + capabilities），这是Neuron容器的常见做法。如果不行，再用 `--privileged`。

你那边有官方DLC的启动说明吗？

message.content: 全都可以参照这个来做：https://github.com/aws-neuron/deep-learning-containers#vllm-inference-neuronx

content.thinking: 用户提供了官方文档链接。我需要去获取这个文档，看看AWS官方推荐的DLC启动方式。

content.text: 让我直接查看你本地是否已有DLC相关文档。

content.content:
1	# Neuron Docker Quick Start Guide
2	
3	This guide shows how to use the AWS Neuron Deep Learning Container to simplify Neuron environment management.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Docker installed on Trn2 instance
8	- Access to Neuron devices (`/dev/neuron*`)
9	- Sufficient disk space for model weights and compiled artifacts
10	
11	## Quick Start
12	
13	### 1. Pull the Container
14	
15	```bash
16	docker pull public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.16.0-neuronx-py312-sdk2.30.0-ubuntu24.04
17	```
18	
19	### 2. Verify Neuron Devices
20	
21	```bash
22	ls -l /dev/neuron*
23	neuron-ls  # From host system
24	```
25	
26	### 3. Compile Tongyi Model
27	
28	```bash
29	# Compile with default settings (TP=8, 128K context)
30	bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash
31	
32	# Compile with custom settings
33	TP_SIZE=16 MAX_MODEL_LEN=128000 \
34	  bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash
35	```
36	
37	Compilation logs are saved to `logs/neuron-debug/tongyi-compile-docker-<timestamp>/`
38	
39	### 4. Serve Tongyi Model
40	
41	```bash
42	# Start Tongyi server on port 6008
43	MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
44	PORT=6008 \
45	TP_SIZE=8 \
46	  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
47	```
48	
49	The server will start in a Docker container and be accessible at `http://127.0.0.1:6008`.
50	
51	### 5. Test the Server
52	
53	```bash
54	# Check model availability
55	curl http://127.0.0.1:6008/v1/models
56	
57	# Send a test request
58	python - <<'PY'
59	from openai import OpenAI
60	client = OpenAI(base_url="http://127.0.0.1:6008/v1", api_key="EMPTY")
61	response = client.chat.completions.create(
62	    model="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B",
63	    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is AWS Neuron?"}],
64	    max_tokens=100
65	)
66	print(response.choices[0].message.content)
67	PY
68	```
69	
70	## Docker Compose Alternative
71	
72	For easier management, use Docker Compose:
73	
74	```bash
75	# Start the container
76	docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml up -d neuron-vllm
77	
78	# Check container status
79	docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml ps
80	
81	# View logs
82	docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml logs -f neuron-vllm
83	
84	# Execute commands inside container
85	docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml exec neuron-vllm neuron-ls
86	
87	# Stop the container
88	docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml down
89	```
90	
91	## Running Full Benchmark
92	
93	After compilation, run the full benchmark using the native client scripts (no changes needed):
94	
95	```bash
96	# Activate the client environment
97	source .venv/bin/activate
98	
99	# Run Tongyi benchmark (clients connect to Docker-hosted server via HTTP)
100	bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
101	```
102	
103	The client scripts automatically detect the vLLM server running on localhost, whether it's native or Docker-based.
104	
105	## Volume Mounts
106	
107	The Docker container mounts these directories:
108	
109	- **`local-models/`**: Model weights and compiled artifacts (read-write)
110	- **`logs/`**: Server and compilation logs (read-write)
111	- **`data/`**: Dataset files (read-only)
112	- **`topics-qrels/`**: Query and qrel files (read-only)
113	
114	All paths are preserved, so compiled artifacts from native setups are compatible with Docker and vice versa.
115	
116	## Environment Variables
117	
118	### Common Settings
119	
120	| Variable | Default | Description |
121	|----------|---------|-------------|
122	| `MODEL` | (required) | Hugging Face model ID or local path |
123	| `PORT` | 8000 | HTTP server port |
124	| `TP_SIZE` | 4 | Tensor parallel size (number of NeuronCores) |
125	| `MAX_MODEL_LEN` | 4096 | Maximum sequence length |
126	| `MAX_NUM_SEQS` | 1 | Maximum concurrent sequences |
127	| `NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES` | 0-7 | Visible Neuron cores |
128	
129	### Tongyi-Specific Settings
130	
131	| Variable | Default | Description |
132	|----------|---------|-------------|
133	| `NEURON_SCHEDULER` | custom | `custom` or `native` scheduler |
134	| `ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING` | 1 | Enable automatic prefix caching |
135	| `ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL` | 0 (custom), 1 (native) | Enable chunked prefill |
136	| `NEURON_CACHE_TAG` | (auto) | Manual cache invalidation tag |
137	
138	## Compilation Artifacts
139	
140	Compiled models are stored in:
141	```
142	local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/<config-hash>/
143	  ├── model.pt                    # Compiled model
144	  ├── neuron_config.json          # Configuration
145	  └── weights/                    # Pre-sharded weights (if enabled)
146	      ├── tp0_sharded_checkpoint.safetensors
147	      ├── tp1_sharded_checkpoint.safetensors
148	      └── ...
149	```
150	
151	The configuration hash includes model ID, TP size, sequence length, scheduler type, and cache tag. Changing any parameter creates a new artifact directory.
152	
153	## Multiple Models
154	
155	To run multiple models simultaneously, use different ports and core ranges:
156	
157	```bash
158	# Tongyi replica 1 (cores 0-7, port 6008)
159	NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=0-7 PORT=6008 MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
160	  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &
161	
162	# Tongyi replica 2 (cores 8-15, port 6009)
163	NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=8-15 PORT=6009 MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
164	  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &
165	
166	# Judge model (cores 48-55, port 8032)
167	NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=48-55 PORT=8032 MODEL="Qwen/Qwen3-32B" TP_SIZE=8 \
168	  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &
169	```
170	
171	Each server runs in its own container with isolated Neuron cores.
172	
173	## Troubleshooting
174	
175	### Container Cannot Access Neuron Devices
176	
177	**Symptom**: `neuron-ls` returns no devices inside container
178	
179	**Solution**: Ensure Docker has access to Neuron devices:
180	```bash
181	# Check host
182	ls -l /dev/neuron*
183	
184	# Add --device flags explicitly
185	docker run --device=/dev/neuron0 --device=/dev/neuron1 ...
186	```
187	
188	### Compilation Fails with Memory Error
189	
190	**Symptom**: `NCC_EOOM002` error during compilation
191	
192	**Solution**: Compile sequentially with `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1`:
193	```bash
194	NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash
195	```
196	
197	### Port Already in Use
198	
199	**Symptom**: `Address already in use` error
200	
201	**Solution**: Check for existing servers:
202	```bash
203	# List running vLLM containers
204	docker ps | grep vllm
205	
206	# Stop specific container
207	docker stop <container-name>
208	
209	# Or use a different port
210	PORT=6009 bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
211	```
212	
213	### Cannot Find Compiled Artifacts
214	
215	**Symptom**: Server recompiles on every start
216	
217	**Solution**: Check volume mounts and cache paths:
218	```bash
219	# Verify artifacts exist
220	ls -la local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/
221	
222	# Check Docker mount
223	docker inspect <container-name> | grep -A 10 Mounts
224	```
225	
226	## Differences from Native Setup
227	
228	| Aspect | Native | Docker |
229	|--------|--------|--------|
230	| Python Version | 3.10 | 3.12 |
231	| vLLM Version | 0.16.0 | 0.16.0 |
232	| Neuron SDK | Manual install | Pre-installed 2.30.0 |
233	| Environment | `.venv-neuron` | Container |
234	| Startup Time | Faster (no container overhead) | Slightly slower (~1-2s) |
235	| Isolation | Shared host packages | Isolated container |
236	| Artifact Compatibility | ✅ Compatible | ✅ Compatible |
237	
238	## Next Steps
239	
240	- Read the full [Migration Plan](neuron_docker_migration.md)
241	- See [neuron_trn2.md](neuron_trn2.md) for detailed Neuron configuration
242	- Check [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for project conventions
243	
244	## Support
245	
246	- AWS Neuron Documentation: https://awsdocs-neuron.readthedocs-hosted.com/
247	- vLLM Neuron Guide: https://awsdocs-neuron.readthedocs-hosted.com/en/latest/libraries/nxd-inference/developer_guides/vllm-user-guide.html
248	- Container Repository: https://github.com/aws-neuron/deep-learning-containers

file.content:
# Neuron Docker Quick Start Guide

This guide shows how to use the AWS Neuron Deep Learning Container to simplify Neuron environment management.

## Prerequisites

- Docker installed on Trn2 instance
- Access to Neuron devices (`/dev/neuron*`)
- Sufficient disk space for model weights and compiled artifacts

## Quick Start

### 1. Pull the Container

```bash
docker pull public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.16.0-neuronx-py312-sdk2.30.0-ubuntu24.04
```

### 2. Verify Neuron Devices

```bash
ls -l /dev/neuron*
neuron-ls  # From host system
```

### 3. Compile Tongyi Model

```bash
# Compile with default settings (TP=8, 128K context)
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash

# Compile with custom settings
TP_SIZE=16 MAX_MODEL_LEN=128000 \
  bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash
```

Compilation logs are saved to `logs/neuron-debug/tongyi-compile-docker-<timestamp>/`

### 4. Serve Tongyi Model

```bash
# Start Tongyi server on port 6008
MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
PORT=6008 \
TP_SIZE=8 \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
```

The server will start in a Docker container and be accessible at `http://127.0.0.1:6008`.

### 5. Test the Server

```bash
# Check model availability
curl http://127.0.0.1:6008/v1/models

# Send a test request
python - <<'PY'
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI(base_url="http://127.0.0.1:6008/v1", api_key="EMPTY")
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is AWS Neuron?"}],
    max_tokens=100
)
print(response.choices[0].message.content)
PY
```

## Docker Compose Alternative

For easier management, use Docker Compose:

```bash
# Start the container
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml up -d neuron-vllm

# Check container status
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml ps

# View logs
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml logs -f neuron-vllm

# Execute commands inside container
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml exec neuron-vllm neuron-ls

# Stop the container
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml down
```

## Running Full Benchmark

After compilation, run the full benchmark using the native client scripts (no changes needed):

```bash
# Activate the client environment
source .venv/bin/activate

# Run Tongyi benchmark (clients connect to Docker-hosted server via HTTP)
bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
```

The client scripts automatically detect the vLLM server running on localhost, whether it's native or Docker-based.

## Volume Mounts

The Docker container mounts these directories:

- **`local-models/`**: Model weights and compiled artifacts (read-write)
- **`logs/`**: Server and compilation logs (read-write)
- **`data/`**: Dataset files (read-only)
- **`topics-qrels/`**: Query and qrel files (read-only)

All paths are preserved, so compiled artifacts from native setups are compatible with Docker and vice versa.

## Environment Variables

### Common Settings

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `MODEL` | (required) | Hugging Face model ID or local path |
| `PORT` | 8000 | HTTP server port |
| `TP_SIZE` | 4 | Tensor parallel size (number of NeuronCores) |
| `MAX_MODEL_LEN` | 4096 | Maximum sequence length |
| `MAX_NUM_SEQS` | 1 | Maximum concurrent sequences |
| `NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES` | 0-7 | Visible Neuron cores |

### Tongyi-Specific Settings

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `NEURON_SCHEDULER` | custom | `custom` or `native` scheduler |
| `ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING` | 1 | Enable automatic prefix caching |
| `ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL` | 0 (custom), 1 (native) | Enable chunked prefill |
| `NEURON_CACHE_TAG` | (auto) | Manual cache invalidation tag |

## Compilation Artifacts

Compiled models are stored in:
```
local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/<config-hash>/
  ├── model.pt                    # Compiled model
  ├── neuron_config.json          # Configuration
  └── weights/                    # Pre-sharded weights (if enabled)
      ├── tp0_sharded_checkpoint.safetensors
      ├── tp1_sharded_checkpoint.safetensors
      └── ...
```

The configuration hash includes model ID, TP size, sequence length, scheduler type, and cache tag. Changing any parameter creates a new artifact directory.

## Multiple Models

To run multiple models simultaneously, use different ports and core ranges:

```bash
# Tongyi replica 1 (cores 0-7, port 6008)
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=0-7 PORT=6008 MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &

# Tongyi replica 2 (cores 8-15, port 6009)
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=8-15 PORT=6009 MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &

# Judge model (cores 48-55, port 8032)
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=48-55 PORT=8032 MODEL="Qwen/Qwen3-32B" TP_SIZE=8 \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &
```

Each server runs in its own container with isolated Neuron cores.

## Troubleshooting

### Container Cannot Access Neuron Devices

**Symptom**: `neuron-ls` returns no devices inside container

**Solution**: Ensure Docker has access to Neuron devices:
```bash
# Check host
ls -l /dev/neuron*

# Add --device flags explicitly
docker run --device=/dev/neuron0 --device=/dev/neuron1 ...
```

### Compilation Fails with Memory Error

**Symptom**: `NCC_EOOM002` error during compilation

**Solution**: Compile sequentially with `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1`:
```bash
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash
```

### Port Already in Use

**Symptom**: `Address already in use` error

**Solution**: Check for existing servers:
```bash
# List running vLLM containers
docker ps | grep vllm

# Stop specific container
docker stop <container-name>

# Or use a different port
PORT=6009 bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
```

### Cannot Find Compiled Artifacts

**Symptom**: Server recompiles on every start

**Solution**: Check volume mounts and cache paths:
```bash
# Verify artifacts exist
ls -la local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/

# Check Docker mount
docker inspect <container-name> | grep -A 10 Mounts
```

## Differences from Native Setup

| Aspect | Native | Docker |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Python Version | 3.10 | 3.12 |
| vLLM Version | 0.16.0 | 0.16.0 |
| Neuron SDK | Manual install | Pre-installed 2.30.0 |
| Environment | `.venv-neuron` | Container |
| Startup Time | Faster (no container overhead) | Slightly slower (~1-2s) |
| Isolation | Shared host packages | Isolated container |
| Artifact Compatibility | ✅ Compatible | ✅ Compatible |

## Next Steps

- Read the full [Migration Plan](neuron_docker_migration.md)
- See [neuron_trn2.md](neuron_trn2.md) for detailed Neuron configuration
- Check [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for project conventions

## Support

- AWS Neuron Documentation: https://awsdocs-neuron.readthedocs-hosted.com/
- vLLM Neuron Guide: https://awsdocs-neuron.readthedocs-hosted.com/en/latest/libraries/nxd-inference/developer_guides/vllm-user-guide.html
- Container Repository: https://github.com/aws-neuron/deep-learning-containers
