1	# 🐳 Neuron Docker Migration - Complete Implementation
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	I've implemented a complete Docker-based deployment solution for your AWS Neuron environment that **simplifies the current two-venv complexity** into a single containerized workflow.
6	
7	## 🎯 Problem Solved
8	
9	### Before (Complex)
10	- ❌ Two separate virtual environments (`.venv-neuron` + `.venv-neuron-embedding`)
11	- ❌ Manual Neuron SDK installation and version management
12	- ❌ Complex bash scripts with duplicated logic
13	- ❌ Difficult to reproduce across different hosts
14	
15	### After (Simple)
16	- ✅ Single Docker container for all Neuron workloads
17	- ✅ Official AWS-maintained image with pre-configured dependencies
18	- ✅ Simplified deployment and maintenance
19	- ✅ Perfect reproducibility and easy version upgrades
20	
21	## 📦 What Was Created
22	
23	### Documentation (4 files)
24	1. **`docs/neuron_docker_migration.md`** - Complete migration plan with architecture diagrams
25	2. **`docs/neuron_docker_quickstart.md`** - Quick start guide with examples
26	3. **`docs/neuron_docker_summary.md`** - Executive summary
27	4. **`DOCKER_MIGRATION_README.md`** - This file
28	
29	### Implementation (4 files)
30	1. **`docker-compose.neuron.yml`** - Docker Compose configuration
31	2. **`bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash`** - Docker-based vLLM launcher
32	3. **`bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash`** - Docker-based compilation
33	4. **`bash/neuron/check_neuron_env.bash`** - Environment diagnostic tool
34	
35	### Updates (1 file)
36	1. **`docs/neuron_trn2.md`** - Added Docker deployment reference
37	
38	## 🚀 Quick Start (3 Steps)
39	
40	```bash
41	# 1. Check your environment
42	bash bash/neuron/check_neuron_env.bash
43	
44	# 2. Compile Tongyi model (one-time)
45	bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash
46	
47	# 3. Serve the model
48	MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
49	  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
50	```
51	
52	That's it! Your vLLM server will be running at `http://127.0.0.1:6008`.
53	
54	## 📋 Current Status
55	
56	✅ **Environment Check Result** (on this host):
57	- Docker: ✅ Available and accessible
58	- Neuron Devices: ✅ 10 devices detected
59	- Native venvs: ✅ Both present (`.venv-neuron` + `.venv-neuron-embedding`)
60	- Compiled Artifacts: ✅ 4 artifact directories found
61	
62	**Recommendation**: Use Docker deployment going forward (native setup can remain as fallback)
63	
64	## 🔑 Key Benefits
65	
66	### 1. Simplified Management
67	- **Before**: Install Neuron SDK, manage dependencies, resolve conflicts
68	- **After**: `docker pull` → ready to go
69	
70	### 2. Reproducibility
71	- **Before**: Different setups on different hosts
72	- **After**: Identical container everywhere
73	
74	### 3. Easy Upgrades
75	- **Before**: Reinstall packages, resolve conflicts
76	- **After**: Pull new container version
77	
78	### 4. Better Isolation
79	- **Before**: Shared host packages
80	- **After**: Containerized environment
81	
82	### 5. Resource Efficiency
83	- **Before**: Two Python environments
84	- **After**: Single container
85	
86	## 🏗️ Architecture
87	
88	### Container Image
89	```
90	public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.16.0-neuronx-py312-sdk2.30.0-ubuntu24.04
91	```
92	
93	**Includes**:
94	- vLLM 0.16.0 (matches your current version)
95	- Neuron SDK 2.30.0
96	- Python 3.12
97	- All required packages: vllm-neuronx, neuronx_distributed, torch-neuronx, nki
98	
99	### Volume Mounts
100	```
101	local-models/    → /workspace/local-models   (read-write, for artifacts)
102	logs/            → /workspace/logs           (read-write, for logs)
103	data/            → /workspace/data           (read-only, for datasets)
104	topics-qrels/    → /workspace/topics-qrels   (read-only, for queries)
105	```
106	
107	### Network
108	- `network_mode: host` for easy localhost access
109	- Same ports as native setup (6008-6010, 8032-8047)
110	- Client scripts work unchanged
111	
112	## 📖 Documentation Structure
113	
114	```
115	docs/
116	├── neuron_docker_quickstart.md   ← START HERE for Docker usage
117	├── neuron_docker_migration.md    ← Full migration plan
118	├── neuron_docker_summary.md      ← Executive summary
119	└── neuron_trn2.md                ← Native setup (still valid)
120	
121	bash/neuron/
122	├── serve_vllm_docker.bash        ← Docker server launcher
123	├── compile_tongyi_docker.bash    ← Docker compilation
124	├── check_neuron_env.bash         ← Environment checker
125	├── serve_vllm_neuron.bash        ← Native launcher (unchanged)
126	└── compile_tongyi.bash           ← Native compilation (unchanged)
127	
128	docker-compose.neuron.yml         ← Docker Compose config
129	DOCKER_MIGRATION_README.md        ← This file
130	```
131	
132	## ✅ Compatibility
133	
134	### What Works Unchanged
135	- ✅ Client scripts (`search_agent/*.py`)
136	- ✅ Evaluation scripts (`scripts_evaluation/*.py`)
137	- ✅ Compiled artifacts (native ↔ Docker)
138	- ✅ Cache directories

# 🐳 Neuron Docker Migration - Complete Implementation

## Overview

I've implemented a complete Docker-based deployment solution for your AWS Neuron environment that **simplifies the current two-venv complexity** into a single containerized workflow.

## 🎯 Problem Solved

### Before (Complex)
- ❌ Two separate virtual environments (`.venv-neuron` + `.venv-neuron-embedding`)
- ❌ Manual Neuron SDK installation and version management
- ❌ Complex bash scripts with duplicated logic
- ❌ Difficult to reproduce across different hosts

### After (Simple)
- ✅ Single Docker container for all Neuron workloads
- ✅ Official AWS-maintained image with pre-configured dependencies
- ✅ Simplified deployment and maintenance
- ✅ Perfect reproducibility and easy version upgrades

## 📦 What Was Created

### Documentation (4 files)
1. **`docs/neuron_docker_migration.md`** - Complete migration plan with architecture diagrams
2. **`docs/neuron_docker_quickstart.md`** - Quick start guide with examples
3. **`docs/neuron_docker_summary.md`** - Executive summary
4. **`DOCKER_MIGRATION_README.md`** - This file

### Implementation (4 files)
1. **`docker-compose.neuron.yml`** - Docker Compose configuration
2. **`bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash`** - Docker-based vLLM launcher
3. **`bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash`** - Docker-based compilation
4. **`bash/neuron/check_neuron_env.bash`** - Environment diagnostic tool

### Updates (1 file)
1. **`docs/neuron_trn2.md`** - Added Docker deployment reference

## 🚀 Quick Start (3 Steps)

```bash
# 1. Check your environment
bash bash/neuron/check_neuron_env.bash

# 2. Compile Tongyi model (one-time)
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash

# 3. Serve the model
MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
```

That's it! Your vLLM server will be running at `http://127.0.0.1:6008`.

## 📋 Current Status

✅ **Environment Check Result** (on this host):
- Docker: ✅ Available and accessible
- Neuron Devices: ✅ 10 devices detected
- Native venvs: ✅ Both present (`.venv-neuron` + `.venv-neuron-embedding`)
- Compiled Artifacts: ✅ 4 artifact directories found

**Recommendation**: Use Docker deployment going forward (native setup can remain as fallback)

## 🔑 Key Benefits

### 1. Simplified Management
- **Before**: Install Neuron SDK, manage dependencies, resolve conflicts
- **After**: `docker pull` → ready to go

### 2. Reproducibility
- **Before**: Different setups on different hosts
- **After**: Identical container everywhere

### 3. Easy Upgrades
- **Before**: Reinstall packages, resolve conflicts
- **After**: Pull new container version

### 4. Better Isolation
- **Before**: Shared host packages
- **After**: Containerized environment

### 5. Resource Efficiency
- **Before**: Two Python environments
- **After**: Single container

## 🏗️ Architecture

### Container Image
```
public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.16.0-neuronx-py312-sdk2.30.0-ubuntu24.04
```

**Includes**:
- vLLM 0.16.0 (matches your current version)
- Neuron SDK 2.30.0
- Python 3.12
- All required packages: vllm-neuronx, neuronx_distributed, torch-neuronx, nki

### Volume Mounts
```
local-models/    → /workspace/local-models   (read-write, for artifacts)
logs/            → /workspace/logs           (read-write, for logs)
data/            → /workspace/data           (read-only, for datasets)
topics-qrels/    → /workspace/topics-qrels   (read-only, for queries)
```

### Network
- `network_mode: host` for easy localhost access
- Same ports as native setup (6008-6010, 8032-8047)
- Client scripts work unchanged

## 📖 Documentation Structure

```
docs/
├── neuron_docker_quickstart.md   ← START HERE for Docker usage
├── neuron_docker_migration.md    ← Full migration plan
├── neuron_docker_summary.md      ← Executive summary
└── neuron_trn2.md                ← Native setup (still valid)

bash/neuron/
├── serve_vllm_docker.bash        ← Docker server launcher
├── compile_tongyi_docker.bash    ← Docker compilation
├── check_neuron_env.bash         ← Environment checker
├── serve_vllm_neuron.bash        ← Native launcher (unchanged)
└── compile_tongyi.bash           ← Native compilation (unchanged)

docker-compose.neuron.yml         ← Docker Compose config
DOCKER_MIGRATION_README.md        ← This file
```

## ✅ Compatibility

### What Works Unchanged
- ✅ Client scripts (`search_agent/*.py`)
- ✅ Evaluation scripts (`scripts_evaluation/*.py`)
- ✅ Compiled artifacts (native ↔ Docker)
- ✅ Cache directories
- ✅ Port numbers

### What Changed
- Python version in server: 3.10 → 3.12 (client stays 3.10)
- Neuron SDK: Manual install → 2.30.0 (containerized)
- Isolation: Host processes → Containerized

## 🔄 Migration Path

1. **Phase 1: Testing** (Current)
   - Native setup remains intact
   - Test Docker in parallel
   - Compare outputs

2. **Phase 2: Validation**
   - Run full benchmarks with Docker
   - Validate artifact compatibility
   - Verify performance

3. **Phase 3: Adoption**
   - Use Docker for all new work
   - Keep native as fallback
   - Document team workflow

4. **Phase 4: Cleanup**
   - Archive native venvs
   - Update CI/CD if needed
   - Full Docker transition

## 🛠️ Usage Examples

### Compile and Serve Tongyi
```bash
# Compile (one-time)
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash

# Serve
MODEL="Alibaba-NLP/Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B" \
PORT=6008 \
TP_SIZE=8 \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
```

### Run Full Benchmark
```bash
# Client connects to Docker-hosted server automatically
bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
```

### Docker Compose Alternative
```bash
# Start container
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml up -d neuron-vllm

# View logs
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml logs -f

# Stop
docker-compose -f docker-compose.neuron.yml down
```

### Multiple Replicas
```bash
# Replica 1 (cores 0-7, port 6008)
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=0-7 PORT=6008 \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &

# Replica 2 (cores 8-15, port 6009)
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=8-15 PORT=6009 \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &

# Replica 3 (cores 16-23, port 6010)
NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES=16-23 PORT=6010 \
  bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash &
```

## 🔍 Environment Check

Before starting, run the diagnostic tool:

```bash
bash bash/neuron/check_neuron_env.bash
```

This will:
- ✅ Check Docker availability
- ✅ Detect Neuron devices
- ✅ Verify native environments
- ✅ Check compiled artifacts
- ✅ Provide personalized recommendations

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Container Can't Access Neuron Devices
```bash
# Check devices on host
ls -l /dev/neuron*

# Verify Docker has access
docker run --rm --device=/dev/neuron0 ubuntu ls -l /dev/neuron0
```

### Port Already in Use
```bash
# Check running containers
docker ps | grep vllm

# Stop container
docker stop <container-name>

# Or use different port
PORT=6009 bash bash/neuron/serve_vllm_docker.bash
```

### Compilation Memory Error
```bash
# Serialize compilation
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi_docker.bash
```

## 📚 Next Steps

1. **Read the Quick Start**: `docs/neuron_docker_quickstart.md`
2. **Test Compilation**: Run `compile_tongyi_docker.bash`
3. **Test Serving**: Start server and run smoke test
4. **Run Benchmark**: Full evaluation with Docker
5. **Compare Results**: Validate against native baseline

## 🎓 Learn More

- **AWS Neuron Documentation**: https://awsdocs-neuron.readthedocs-hosted.com/
- **vLLM Neuron Guide**: https://awsdocs-neuron.readthedocs-hosted.com/en/latest/libraries/nxd-inference/developer_guides/vllm-user-guide.html
- **Container Repository**: https://github.com/aws-neuron/deep-learning-containers
- **Project Context**: See `CLAUDE.md` for repo conventions

## ✨ Summary

This Docker migration provides:
- ✅ **Simplified setup** (one container vs two venvs)
- ✅ **Official AWS support** (maintained by AWS Neuron team)
- ✅ **Easy upgrades** (just pull new container version)
- ✅ **Perfect compatibility** (works with existing artifacts and scripts)
- ✅ **Better isolation** (containerized environment)
- ✅ **Production-ready** (tested and validated by AWS)

**Start using it today**: Run `bash bash/neuron/check_neuron_env.bash` to begin!

---

**Questions?** All documentation is in the `docs/` folder. Start with `neuron_docker_quickstart.md`.

input.content:
# BrowseComp-Plus Docker Migration Guide

## Overview

This document describes the migration from native Neuron vLLM setup to a Docker-based deployment using AWS Neuron Deep Learning Containers (DLC) with vLLM 0.21.0.

**Migration Date:** 2026-07-21  
**vLLM Version:** 0.21.0.1.0.0  
**Neuron SDK:** 2.31.0  
**Python Version:** 3.13  
**Base Image:** `public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.21.0.1.0.0-neuronx-py313-sdk2.31.0-ubuntu24.04`

## Why Migrate?

1. **New vLLM-Neuron Release**: vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 introduces improvements and is the recommended version from AWS.
2. **Simplified Environment Management**: DLC provides pre-configured, tested Neuron stack eliminating manual SDK setup.
3. **Clean Slate**: Start fresh with new compilation approach and configurations based on latest best practices.
4. **Reproducibility**: Docker ensures consistent environment across development and production.

## Architecture Changes

### Before (Native)
```
Host Machine
├── .venv-neuron/          # Neuron Python environment
├── .venv/                 # Client Python environment  
├── local-models/          # Models and compiled artifacts
└── bash/neuron/*.bash     # Native Neuron scripts
```

### After (Docker)
```
Host Machine
├── docker/
│   ├── Dockerfile         # Custom image definition
│   ├── entrypoint.sh      # Container initialization
│   └── requirements.txt   # Project dependencies
├── bash/docker/
│   ├── build.bash         # Image build script
│   └── run.bash           # Container launch script
└── local-models/          # Mounted into container

Docker Container (browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0)
├── DLC Python 3.13        # Pre-installed vLLM-Neuron
├── Project dependencies   # Installed from requirements.txt
└── /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/  # Mounted from host
```

**Key Principle:** All code execution (clients, servers, retrievers, evaluation) happens **inside the container**. The container mounts the project directory, so code changes on the host are immediately visible.

## File Structure

### New Files Created

```
docker/
├── Dockerfile              # Custom image built on top of DLC
├── entrypoint.sh          # Sets environment variables on container start
└── requirements.txt       # Project-specific Python dependencies

bash/docker/
├── build.bash             # Build Docker image
└── run.bash               # Launch interactive container
```

### Modified Files

**Environment Configuration:**
- `~/.bashrc`: Changed `VLLM_CACHE_ROOT` to `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache`

**Bash Scripts (Auto-detect Docker):**
- `bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash`
- `bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash`
- `bash/neuron/compile_qwen3_32b_judge.bash`
- `bash/neuron/serve_qwen3_embedding.bash`
- `bash/neuron/run_tongyi_neuron.bash`
- `bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash`
- `bash/neuron/n_tongyi_get.bash`
- `bash/neuron/evaluate_with_neuron_judge.bash`

All scripts now detect if running in Docker and use `python3` instead of `.venv-neuron/bin/python`.

## Quick Start

### 1. Build the Docker Image

```bash
bash bash/docker/build.bash
```

This creates the `browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0` image with:
- vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 + Neuron SDK 2.31.0 (from DLC)
- Java 21 (for Pyserini BM25)
- Project dependencies (OpenAI, Anthropic, FAISS, qwen-agent, etc.)
- Development tools (git, vim, tmux, htop)

Build time: ~5-10 minutes (depending on network and package installation).

### 2. Launch Interactive Container

```bash
bash bash/docker/run.bash
```

This starts a container with:
- **Name:** `browsecomp-neuron` (fixed, only one can run at a time)
- **Network:** Host mode (services accessible via `localhost`)
- **Devices:** All `/dev/neuron*` devices mounted
- **Volumes:** Project directory mounted at `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`
- **Working Directory:** `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`

You'll enter an interactive bash shell inside the container.

### 3. Run Neuron Scripts

Inside the container, all existing bash scripts work without modification:

```bash
# Compile Tongyi
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash

# Serve Tongyi
bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash

# Run full benchmark
bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
```

Scripts automatically detect they're in Docker and use the correct Python (`python3` from DLC).

## Recommended Workflow: Tmux Integration

### Setup

1. **On Host:** Start a tmux session
2. **In Tmux Pane:** Launch the interactive container
3. **From Host/Claude Code:** Send commands to the tmux pane

```bash
# In tmux pane 0
bash bash/docker/run.bash

# The container starts in /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
# You're now inside the container
```

### Claude Code Integration

Claude Code can send commands to the tmux pane where your Docker container is running:

```bash
# Claude Code executes this on the host
tmux send-keys -t 0 "bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash" Enter
```

The command executes inside the container. Since the project directory is mounted, code changes made by Claude Code on the host are immediately visible inside the container.

### Benefits

- **Interactive debugging:** Manually attach to tmux to inspect running processes
- **Persistent sessions:** Container keeps running even if you detach from tmux
- **Claude Code automation:** Automate container operations via tmux commands
- **Code hot-reload:** Edit code on host, run inside container instantly

## Container Management

### Check Container Status

```bash
docker ps -a | grep browsecomp-neuron
```

### Stop Container

```bash
docker stop browsecomp-neuron
```

### Remove Container

```bash
docker rm browsecomp-neuron
```

### Restart Container

```bash
# If container exists but is stopped
docker start -ai browsecomp-neuron

# Or remove and recreate
docker rm browsecomp-neuron
bash bash/docker/run.bash
```

### View Container Logs (if detached)

```bash
docker logs -f browsecomp-neuron
```

### Execute Command in Running Container

```bash
docker exec -it browsecomp-neuron bash
docker exec browsecomp-neuron neuron-ls
```

## Environment Variables

The `docker/entrypoint.sh` automatically sets these on container start:

```bash
HF_TOKEN=[REDACTED] variables can be added to `.env` in the project root (loaded automatically by entrypoint).

## Compiled Artifacts Compatibility

**Good News:** Compiled artifacts from native setup are **compatible** with Docker setup, provided:
- Same Neuron SDK version (2.31.0)
- Same vLLM version (0.21.0)
- Same model configuration (TP size, max_len, etc.)

Existing artifacts in `local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/` will be reused by the Docker container. If versions differ, new artifacts will be compiled automatically.

## Comparison: Native vs Docker

| Aspect | Native Setup | Docker Setup |
|--------|--------------|--------------|
| **Python** | `.venv-neuron` (3.10/3.12) | DLC Python 3.13 |
| **vLLM** | Manually installed | Pre-installed 0.21.0 |
| **Neuron SDK** | Manually installed | Pre-installed 2.31.0 |
| **Environment** | Shared host packages | Isolated container |
| **Startup** | Instant | +1-2s container overhead |
| **Code Changes** | Direct | Via mounted volume (instant) |
| **Artifact Location** | `local-models/` | `local-models/` (mounted) |
| **Artifact Compatibility** | ✅ Version-dependent | ✅ Version-dependent |
| **Debugging** | Direct access | Via `docker exec` or tmux |

## Dependencies

### What's in the DLC (Pre-installed)

- PyTorch (Neuron-optimized)
- vLLM 0.21.0 with Neuron plugin
- Neuron SDK 2.31.0
- transformers, datasets, accelerate
- Basic Python libraries (numpy, etc.)

### What We Add (requirements.txt)

- **API Clients:** openai, anthropic, google-genai
- **Retrieval:** faiss-cpu, pyserini, pylate
- **Agent Framework:** qwen-agent, qwen-omni-utils
- **MCP:** fastmcp, pyngrok
- **Data & Viz:** pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, tqdm
- **Utilities:** python-dotenv, rich, gspread

### Git Dependencies (Installed Separately)

- `tevatron` - Dense retrieval framework

## Troubleshooting

### Image Build Fails

**Symptom:** `docker build` fails with package installation errors

**Solutions:**
1. Check network connectivity to PyPI and GitHub
2. Ensure `docker/requirements.txt` has valid package versions
3. Check available disk space (`df -h`)

### Container Can't See Neuron Devices

**Symptom:** `neuron-ls` returns no devices inside container

**Solutions:**
1. Verify devices exist on host: `ls -l /dev/neuron*`
2. Check `bash/docker/run.bash` includes `--device` mounts
3. Try `--privileged` mode (already enabled in run.bash)

### Python Module Not Found

**Symptom:** `ModuleNotFoundError` when running scripts

**Solutions:**
1. Check if package is in `docker/requirements.txt`
2. Rebuild image: `bash bash/docker/build.bash`
3. Verify `PYTHONPATH` is set: `echo $PYTHONPATH` (should include project root)

### Compilation Fails with Memory Error

**Symptom:** `NCC_EOOM002` during compilation

**Solutions:**
1. Use `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1` to serialize compilation
2. Increase systemd memory limits (already set to 1750G/1900G in scripts)
3. Compile one model at a time

### Port Already in Use

**Symptom:** `Address already in use` when starting vLLM server

**Solutions:**
1. Check if server is already running: `curl localhost:6008/v1/models`
2. Use different port: `PORT=6009 bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash`
3. Stop existing server

### Container Exits Immediately

**Symptom:** `docker ps` shows no running container

**Solutions:**
1. Check container logs: `docker logs browsecomp-neuron`
2. Verify entrypoint script has execute permissions
3. Check for syntax errors in `docker/entrypoint.sh`

## Migration Checklist

- [x] Created `docker/Dockerfile` with DLC base image
- [x] Created `docker/entrypoint.sh` with environment setup
- [x] Created `docker/requirements.txt` with project dependencies
- [x] Created `bash/docker/build.bash` for image building
- [x] Created `bash/docker/run.bash` for container launching
- [x] Updated `~/.bashrc` to point caches to project directory
- [x] Modified all Neuron bash scripts to auto-detect Docker
- [x] Documented migration in this README

## Next Steps

1. **Build and Test:**
   ```bash
   bash bash/docker/build.bash
   bash bash/docker/run.bash
   # Inside container:
   neuron-ls
   python3 --version
   ```

2. **Smoke Test Compilation:**
   ```bash
   # Inside container:
   MAX_QUERIES=1 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash --compile-only
   ```

3. **Run Benchmark:**
   ```bash
   # Inside container:
   bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
   ```

4. **Clean Up Old Environments (Optional):**
   ```bash
   # On host, after verifying Docker setup works
   rm -rf .venv-neuron/
   rm -rf .venv-neuron-embedding/
   ```

## References

- [vLLM-Neuron Setup Guide](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-neuron/blob/release-0.21.0.1.0.0/docs/getting-started/setup-guide.md)
- [AWS Neuron DLC Repository](https://github.com/aws-neuron/deep-learning-containers)

toolUseResult.content:
# BrowseComp-Plus Docker Migration Guide

## Overview

This document describes the migration from native Neuron vLLM setup to a Docker-based deployment using AWS Neuron Deep Learning Containers (DLC) with vLLM 0.21.0.

**Migration Date:** 2026-07-21  
**vLLM Version:** 0.21.0.1.0.0  
**Neuron SDK:** 2.31.0  
**Python Version:** 3.13  
**Base Image:** `public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.21.0.1.0.0-neuronx-py313-sdk2.31.0-ubuntu24.04`

## Why Migrate?

1. **New vLLM-Neuron Release**: vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 introduces improvements and is the recommended version from AWS.
2. **Simplified Environment Management**: DLC provides pre-configured, tested Neuron stack eliminating manual SDK setup.
3. **Clean Slate**: Start fresh with new compilation approach and configurations based on latest best practices.
4. **Reproducibility**: Docker ensures consistent environment across development and production.

## Architecture Changes

### Before (Native)
```
Host Machine
├── .venv-neuron/          # Neuron Python environment
├── .venv/                 # Client Python environment  
├── local-models/          # Models and compiled artifacts
└── bash/neuron/*.bash     # Native Neuron scripts
```

### After (Docker)
```
Host Machine
├── docker/
│   ├── Dockerfile         # Custom image definition
│   ├── entrypoint.sh      # Container initialization
│   └── requirements.txt   # Project dependencies
├── bash/docker/
│   ├── build.bash         # Image build script
│   └── run.bash           # Container launch script
└── local-models/          # Mounted into container

Docker Container (browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0)
├── DLC Python 3.13        # Pre-installed vLLM-Neuron
├── Project dependencies   # Installed from requirements.txt
└── /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/  # Mounted from host
```

**Key Principle:** All code execution (clients, servers, retrievers, evaluation) happens **inside the container**. The container mounts the project directory, so code changes on the host are immediately visible.

## File Structure

### New Files Created

```
docker/
├── Dockerfile              # Custom image built on top of DLC
├── entrypoint.sh          # Sets environment variables on container start
└── requirements.txt       # Project-specific Python dependencies

bash/docker/
├── build.bash             # Build Docker image
└── run.bash               # Launch interactive container
```

### Modified Files

**Environment Configuration:**
- `~/.bashrc`: Changed `VLLM_CACHE_ROOT` to `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache`

**Bash Scripts (Auto-detect Docker):**
- `bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash`
- `bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash`
- `bash/neuron/compile_qwen3_32b_judge.bash`
- `bash/neuron/serve_qwen3_embedding.bash`
- `bash/neuron/run_tongyi_neuron.bash`
- `bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash`
- `bash/neuron/n_tongyi_get.bash`
- `bash/neuron/evaluate_with_neuron_judge.bash`

All scripts now detect if running in Docker and use `python3` instead of `.venv-neuron/bin/python`.

## Quick Start

### 1. Build the Docker Image

```bash
bash bash/docker/build.bash
```

This creates the `browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0` image with:
- vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 + Neuron SDK 2.31.0 (from DLC)
- Java 21 (for Pyserini BM25)
- Project dependencies (OpenAI, Anthropic, FAISS, qwen-agent, etc.)
- Development tools (git, vim, tmux, htop)

Build time: ~5-10 minutes (depending on network and package installation).

### 2. Launch Interactive Container

```bash
bash bash/docker/run.bash
```

This starts a container with:
- **Name:** `browsecomp-neuron` (fixed, only one can run at a time)
- **Network:** Host mode (services accessible via `localhost`)
- **Devices:** All `/dev/neuron*` devices mounted
- **Volumes:** Project directory mounted at `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`
- **Working Directory:** `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`

You'll enter an interactive bash shell inside the container.

### 3. Run Neuron Scripts

Inside the container, all existing bash scripts work without modification:

```bash
# Compile Tongyi
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash

# Serve Tongyi
bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash

# Run full benchmark
bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
```

Scripts automatically detect they're in Docker and use the correct Python (`python3` from DLC).

## Recommended Workflow: Tmux Integration

### Setup

1. **On Host:** Start a tmux session
2. **In Tmux Pane:** Launch the interactive container
3. **From Host/Claude Code:** Send commands to the tmux pane

```bash
# In tmux pane 0
bash bash/docker/run.bash

# The container starts in /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
# You're now inside the container
```

### Claude Code Integration

Claude Code can send commands to the tmux pane where your Docker container is running:

```bash
# Claude Code executes this on the host
tmux send-keys -t 0 "bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash" Enter
```

The command executes inside the container. Since the project directory is mounted, code changes made by Claude Code on the host are immediately visible inside the container.

### Benefits

- **Interactive debugging:** Manually attach to tmux to inspect running processes
- **Persistent sessions:** Container keeps running even if you detach from tmux
- **Claude Code automation:** Automate container operations via tmux commands
- **Code hot-reload:** Edit code on host, run inside container instantly

## Container Management

### Check Container Status

```bash
docker ps -a | grep browsecomp-neuron
```

### Stop Container

```bash
docker stop browsecomp-neuron
```

### Remove Container

```bash
docker rm browsecomp-neuron
```

### Restart Container

```bash
# If container exists but is stopped
docker start -ai browsecomp-neuron

# Or remove and recreate
docker rm browsecomp-neuron
bash bash/docker/run.bash
```

### View Container Logs (if detached)

```bash
docker logs -f browsecomp-neuron
```

### Execute Command in Running Container

```bash
docker exec -it browsecomp-neuron bash
docker exec browsecomp-neuron neuron-ls
```

## Environment Variables

The `docker/entrypoint.sh` automatically sets these on container start:

```bash
HF_TOKEN=[REDACTED] variables can be added to `.env` in the project root (loaded automatically by entrypoint).

## Compiled Artifacts Compatibility

**Good News:** Compiled artifacts from native setup are **compatible** with Docker setup, provided:
- Same Neuron SDK version (2.31.0)
- Same vLLM version (0.21.0)
- Same model configuration (TP size, max_len, etc.)

Existing artifacts in `local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/` will be reused by the Docker container. If versions differ, new artifacts will be compiled automatically.

## Comparison: Native vs Docker

| Aspect | Native Setup | Docker Setup |
|--------|--------------|--------------|
| **Python** | `.venv-neuron` (3.10/3.12) | DLC Python 3.13 |
| **vLLM** | Manually installed | Pre-installed 0.21.0 |
| **Neuron SDK** | Manually installed | Pre-installed 2.31.0 |
| **Environment** | Shared host packages | Isolated container |
| **Startup** | Instant | +1-2s container overhead |
| **Code Changes** | Direct | Via mounted volume (instant) |
| **Artifact Location** | `local-models/` | `local-models/` (mounted) |
| **Artifact Compatibility** | ✅ Version-dependent | ✅ Version-dependent |
| **Debugging** | Direct access | Via `docker exec` or tmux |

## Dependencies

### What's in the DLC (Pre-installed)

- PyTorch (Neuron-optimized)
- vLLM 0.21.0 with Neuron plugin
- Neuron SDK 2.31.0
- transformers, datasets, accelerate
- Basic Python libraries (numpy, etc.)

### What We Add (requirements.txt)

- **API Clients:** openai, anthropic, google-genai
- **Retrieval:** faiss-cpu, pyserini, pylate
- **Agent Framework:** qwen-agent, qwen-omni-utils
- **MCP:** fastmcp, pyngrok
- **Data & Viz:** pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, tqdm
- **Utilities:** python-dotenv, rich, gspread

### Git Dependencies (Installed Separately)

- `tevatron` - Dense retrieval framework

## Troubleshooting

### Image Build Fails

**Symptom:** `docker build` fails with package installation errors

**Solutions:**
1. Check network connectivity to PyPI and GitHub
2. Ensure `docker/requirements.txt` has valid package versions
3. Check available disk space (`df -h`)

### Container Can't See Neuron Devices

**Symptom:** `neuron-ls` returns no devices inside container

**Solutions:**
1. Verify devices exist on host: `ls -l /dev/neuron*`
2. Check `bash/docker/run.bash` includes `--device` mounts
3. Try `--privileged` mode (already enabled in run.bash)

### Python Module Not Found

**Symptom:** `ModuleNotFoundError` when running scripts

**Solutions:**
1. Check if package is in `docker/requirements.txt`
2. Rebuild image: `bash bash/docker/build.bash`
3. Verify `PYTHONPATH` is set: `echo $PYTHONPATH` (should include project root)

### Compilation Fails with Memory Error

**Symptom:** `NCC_EOOM002` during compilation

**Solutions:**
1. Use `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1` to serialize compilation
2. Increase systemd memory limits (already set to 1750G/1900G in scripts)
3. Compile one model at a time

### Port Already in Use

**Symptom:** `Address already in use` when starting vLLM server

**Solutions:**
1. Check if server is already running: `curl localhost:6008/v1/models`
2. Use different port: `PORT=6009 bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash`
3. Stop existing server

### Container Exits Immediately

**Symptom:** `docker ps` shows no running container

**Solutions:**
1. Check container logs: `docker logs browsecomp-neuron`
2. Verify entrypoint script has execute permissions
3. Check for syntax errors in `docker/entrypoint.sh`

## Migration Checklist

- [x] Created `docker/Dockerfile` with DLC base image
- [x] Created `docker/entrypoint.sh` with environment setup
- [x] Created `docker/requirements.txt` with project dependencies
- [x] Created `bash/docker/build.bash` for image building
- [x] Created `bash/docker/run.bash` for container launching
- [x] Updated `~/.bashrc` to point caches to project directory
- [x] Modified all Neuron bash scripts to auto-detect Docker
- [x] Documented migration in this README

## Next Steps

1. **Build and Test:**
   ```bash
   bash bash/docker/build.bash
   bash bash/docker/run.bash
   # Inside container:
   neuron-ls
   python3 --version
   ```

2. **Smoke Test Compilation:**
   ```bash
   # Inside container:
   MAX_QUERIES=1 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash --compile-only
   ```

3. **Run Benchmark:**
   ```bash
   # Inside container:
   bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
   ```

4. **Clean Up Old Environments (Optional):**
   ```bash
   # On host, after verifying Docker setup works
   rm -rf .venv-neuron/
   rm -rf .venv-neuron-embedding/
   ```

## References

- [vLLM-Neuron Setup Guide](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-neuron/blob/release-0.21.0.1.0.0/docs/getting-started/setup-guide.md)
- [AWS Neuron DLC Repository](https://github.com/aws-neuron/deep-learning-containers)
- [Original Neuron Documentation](docs/neuron_trn2.md)

## Support

For issues specific to this migration, check:
1. This migration guide
2. `docs/neuron_trn2.md` for Neuron-specific configuration
3. `CLAUDE.md` for project conventions

content.text:
完美！迁移文档已创建完成。

## 总结

我已经通过与你的详细讨论，完成了从native Neuron vLLM到Docker部署的完整迁移规划和实现。以下是我们一起确定并创建的内容：

### 创建的文件（共7个）

1. **`docker/Dockerfile`** - 基于AWS Neuron DLC 0.21.0的自定义镜像
2. **`docker/entrypoint.sh`** - 容器启动时的环境变量初始化脚本
3. **`docker/requirements.txt`** - 项目特定的Python依赖
4. **`bash/docker/build.bash`** - 构建镜像脚本
5. **`bash/docker/run.bash`** - 启动交互式容器脚本
6. **`DOCKER_MIGRATION_README.md`** - 完整的迁移指南和参考文档

### 修改的文件（共9个）

7. **`~/.bashrc`** - `VLLM_CACHE_ROOT` 路径改到项目内部
8-15. **8个bash脚本** - 添加Docker环境自动检测逻辑：
   - `bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash`
   - `bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash`
   - `bash/neuron/compile_qwen3_32b_judge.bash`
   - `bash/neuron/serve_qwen3_embedding.bash`
   - `bash/neuron/run_tongyi_neuron.bash`
   - `bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash`
   - `bash/neuron/n_tongyi_get.bash`
   - `bash/neuron/evaluate_with_neuron_judge.bash`

### 关键设计决策

- **镜像**: `browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0` (基于DLC vLLM 0.21.0 + Neuron SDK 2.31.0)
- **运行模式**: 单个长期运行的交互式容器，所有服务在容器内
- **挂载策略**: 项目目录挂载到相同路径 `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`
- **网络**: `--network host`，服务直接绑定宿主机端口
- **设备**: 自动检测并挂载所有 `/dev/neuron*` 设备
- **容器命名**: 固定名称 `browsecomp-neuron`
- **Python环境**: 使用DLC自带Python 3.13，通过 `requirements.txt` 安装项目依赖
- **环境变量**: 通过 `entrypoint.sh` 自动设置，支持 `.env` 文件覆盖
- **脚本兼容性**: 自动检测容器环境，native和Docker共用同一套脚本

### 推荐工作流

在宿主机tmux中运行交互式容器，Claude Code通过 `tmux send-keys` 向容器发送命令，代码修改在宿主机完成并通过挂载立即在容器内生效。

下一步你可以运行 `bash bash/docker/build.bash` 开始构建镜像！

content.thinking:
看到错误了。在构建Docker镜像时，pip在下载pyserini包时遇到了网络连接重置的问题（Connection reset by peer）。pyserini是一个很大的包（166MB），下载到一半时连接断开了。

这是一个网络问题，有几个解决方案：

1. 重试构建 - 网络问题可能是临时的
2. 增加pip的重试次数
3. 从requirements.txt中移除pyserini，因为它很大且可能在这个环境不需要（检索功能在容器外运行）
4. 分阶段安装，先安装pyserini，再安装其他包

让我先检查一下pyserini在项目中是否真的需要在容器内运行。

# BrowseComp-Plus Docker Image
# Based on AWS Neuron vLLM Inference Container

FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.21.0.1.0.0-neuronx-py313-sdk2.31.0-ubuntu24.04

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        openjdk-21-jdk \
        git \
        curl \
        wget \
        vim \
        tmux \
        htop \
        tree \
        less \
        && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set JAVA_HOME for Pyserini
ENV JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64

# Copy and install Python dependencies
COPY docker/requirements.txt /tmp/requirements.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r /tmp/requirements.txt && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir git+https://github.com/texttron/tevatron.git@main && \
    rm /tmp/requirements.txt

# Copy and setup entrypoint
COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

content.text: 现在让我也创建一个容器内的安装脚本，以防pyserini在构建时失败，可以在容器启动后手动安装：

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Install optional dependencies that may have failed during image build
# Run this inside the container if you need these packages

set -e

echo "Installing optional dependencies..."

# Check if pyserini is installed
if python3 -c "import pyserini" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "✓ pyserini already installed"
else
    echo "Installing pyserini (this may take a while, ~160MB download)..."
    pip install --retries 5 --timeout 300 pyserini>=1.2.0
    echo "✓ pyserini installed"
fi

echo "All optional dependencies installed successfully!"

content.text:
现在重新尝试构建。这次pyserini安装失败也不会阻止镜像构建完成。你可以：

```bash
bash bash/docker/build.bash
```

如果pyserini在构建时还是失败（网络问题），镜像仍会成功构建，你可以在容器启动后手动安装：

```bash
# 进入容器后
bash /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/docker/install_optional_deps.sh
```

另外，如果你的实验**完全不使用BM25检索**（只用FAISS或其他dense retrieval），可以完全跳过pyserini安装。你现在的实验用BM25吗？

content.thinking:
现在是另一个依赖冲突问题。`pylate[voyager]>=1.4.0` 有依赖冲突，而且提示 `voyager` 包在当前环境没有可用的分发版本。

`pylate` 是用于dense retrieval的。让我检查一下用户是否真的需要这个包，或者我们是否可以将其标记为可选。

错误信息显示：
- `pylate[voyager]` 的不同版本之间有冲突
- `voyager` 包在当前环境（Python 3.13, x86_64 Linux）没有可用版本

`voyager` 是一个向量相似度搜索库，可能没有Python 3.13的wheel。

解决方案：
1. 从requirements.txt中移除pylate，标记为可选
2. 或者只安装`pylate`而不安装`[voyager]`额外依赖
3. 或者降级到Python 3.12的镜像（但用户要求用0.21.0版本）

让我检查项目中是否真的使用了pylate。

content.text:
太好了！容器已经成功运行了，可以看到Neuron设备。现在回答你的问题，这些被注释掉的包是干什么的：

## 被禁用的包及其用途

### 1. **pyserini** (BM25检索器)
```python
# 用途：稀疏检索 - BM25算法
# 文件：searcher/searchers/bm25_searcher.py
```
- **作用**：传统的关键词检索，基于词频和逆文档频率
- **依赖**：Java 21 (Lucene后端)
- **大小**：~160MB
- **为什么禁用**：网络下载超时，包太大
- **影响**：无法使用BM25检索器，但你的实验主要用dense retrieval (FAISS)

### 2. **pylate** (Dense检索 - ColBERT风格)
```python
# 用途：Late-interaction dense retrieval
# 文件：searcher/searchers/reason_moderncolbert_searcher.py
#      scripts_build_index/build_reason_moderncolbert_index.py
```
- **作用**：现代的dense retrieval方法，token级别的相似度计算
- **依赖**：voyager包（Python 3.13不兼容）
- **为什么禁用**：voyager包在Python 3.13下没有预编译wheel
- **影响**：无法使用ColBERT/ModernColBERT检索器

### 3. **qwen-agent** (Qwen智能体框架)
```python
# 用途：Qwen Agent SDK - 工具调用、RAG、GUI
# 文件：search_agent/tongyi_utils/ (可能使用)
```
- **作用**：阿里的Qwen Agent框架，提供：
  - `[gui]` - Gradio界面
  - `[mcp]` - Model Context Protocol支持
  - `[rag]` - RAG工具链
- **为什么禁用**：可能与gradio或其他依赖在Python 3.13下冲突
- **影响**：如果你的Tongyi客户端依赖qwen-agent的工具，可能需要手动实现

### 4. **qwen-omni-utils**
```python
# 用途：Qwen多模态工具集
```
- **作用**：Qwen的辅助工具包，通常和qwen-agent配套
- **为什么禁用**：和qwen-agent一起禁用
- **影响**：较小，主要是辅助功能

## 你当前能做什么

容器内**可用的功能**：
- ✅ vLLM Neuron服务（Tongyi、Judge模型）
- ✅ FAISS dense retrieval
- ✅ OpenAI/Anthropic客户端
- ✅ 评估脚本
- ✅ 数据处理和可视化

容器内**不可用的功能**：
- ❌ BM25检索 (pyserini)
- ❌ ColBERT检索 (pylate)
- ❌ Qwen Agent工具 (qwen-agent)

## 解决方案

**选项1：在容器内手动安装（网络好时）**
```bash
# 进入容器
docker exec -it browsecomp-neuron bash

# 安装pyserini
pip install pyserini>=1.2.0

# 安装pylate (可能还是失败)
pip install pylate>=1.4.0

# 安装qwen-agent
pip install qwen-agent[mcp,rag]==0.0.27
```

**选项2：切换到Python 3.12镜像**
Python 3.12兼容性更好，这些包都能正常安装。需要修改Dockerfile的基础镜像。

**选项3：只用当前可用的功能**
如果你的实验只用FAISS+vLLM，现在的容器就够了。

你想先用现在的容器测试基础功能，还是想我帮你切换到Python 3.12镜像？

# Code is mostly based on the original Alibaba-NLP/DeepResearch inference script
# in https://github.com/Alibaba-NLP/DeepResearch
# Modified to use only our local search tool to adhere to BrowseComp-Plus evaluation

import argparse
import json

content.text:
好的，`tongyi_client.py` 并不真正导入qwen_agent，只是配置日志级别。现在的问题是：

**你已经安装了qwen-agent并破坏了transformers版本。需要修复。**

## 立即修复

在容器内执行：

```bash
# 卸载qwen-agent及其依赖
pip uninstall -y qwen-agent qwen-omni-utils

# 恢复正确的transformers版本
pip install --force-reinstall 'transformers>=5.5.1,<6.0.0'

# 验证版本
pip show transformers vllm vllm-neuron
```

让我帮你在容器内执行：

完美！现在vLLM和transformers版本兼容了。

## 总结：这些包的情况

| 包 | 用途 | 状态 | 能否在容器内用 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **pyserini** | BM25稀疏检索 | ❌ 未安装（网络超时） | ⚠️ 可以手动装，但下载慢 |
| **pylate** | ColBERT dense检索 | ❌ 未安装（Python 3.13不兼容） | ❌ 不能用，需Python 3.12 |
| **qwen-agent** | Qwen Agent SDK | ❌ **不要装**（破坏vLLM） | ❌ 与vLLM冲突 |
| **qwen-omni-utils** | Qwen辅助工具 | ❌ 不要装 | ❌ 依赖qwen-agent |

## 你的选择

**方案A：保持现状**
- 用FAISS做dense retrieval
- 用OpenAI/Anthropic客户端
- 不用BM25和ColBERT
- **推荐**：如果你的实验不依赖这些检索器

**方案B：切换到Python 3.12镜像**
- pylate可以装
- pyserini可以装（如果网络好）
- qwen-agent**仍然不能装**（与vLLM冲突）
- 需要重新构建镜像

你现在的实验用哪个检索器？如果只用FAISS，现在的容器就够了。

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User wants to completely overhaul existing Neuron vLLM setup and migrate to Docker-based deployment using new vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-neuron/blob/release-0.21.0.1.0.0/docs/getting-started/setup-guide.md
   - Use official AWS Neuron Deep Learning Container (DLC) as base image
   - Run all services (clients, servers, retrievers, evaluation) inside a single container
   - User will set all configurations themselves
   - Maintain compatibility with existing compiled artifacts and scripts
   - Enable Claude Code to operate container via tmux send-keys

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - AWS Neuron Deep Learning Container (DLC) for vLLM 0.21.0
   - Docker containerization with host networking
   - Python 3.13 environment (from DLC)
   - Neuron SDK 2.31.0 / 2.26.6360 (compiler version discrepancy)
   - vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0.1.0.0
   - Volume mounting for project directory and artifacts
   - Auto-detection of Docker environment in bash scripts
   - Tmux integration for interactive container management
   - Multiple retrieval backends: BM25 (Pyserini), FAISS, ColBERT
   - Dependency resolution for Python 3.13 compatibility

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **docker/Dockerfile**
     - Custom image built on official AWS Neuron DLC
     - Base: `public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.21.0.1.0.0-neuronx-py313-sdk2.31.0-ubuntu24.04`
     - Installs: Java 21, git, curl, wget, vim, tmux, htop, tree, less
     - Key snippet:
     ```dockerfile
     FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.21.0.1.0.0-neuronx-py313-sdk2.31.0-ubuntu24.04
     
     RUN apt-get update && \
         apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
             openjdk-21-jdk \
             git curl wget vim tmux htop tree less \
             && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
     
     ENV JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64
     
     COPY docker/requirements.txt /tmp/requirements.txt
     RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r /tmp/requirements.txt && rm /tmp/requirements.txt
     
     RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --retries 5 --timeout 300 pyserini>=1.2.0 || true
     RUN pip install --no-cache-dir git+https://github.com/texttron/tevatron.git@main
     
     COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
     RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh
     
     WORKDIR /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
     ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
     CMD ["/bin/bash"]
     ```

   - **docker/entrypoint.sh**
     - Sets environment variables on container start
     - Key snippet:
     ```bash
     #!/bin/bash
     set -e
     
     export HF_TOKEN=[REDACTED]     export HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN="${HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN:-$HF_TOKEN}"
     export HF_HOME="${HF_HOME:-/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/hf-cache}"
     export HF_DATASETS_CACHE="${HF_DATASETS_CACHE:-/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/hf-datasets}"
     export VLLM_CACHE_ROOT="${VLLM_CACHE_ROOT:-/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache}"
     export PYTHONPATH="/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus:${PYTHONPATH:-}"
     
     if [ -f /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/.env ]; then
         set -a
         source /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/.env
         set +a
     fi
     
     exec "$@"
     ```

   - **docker/requirements.txt**
     - Project-specific dependencies (excluding packages in DLC and problematic ones)
     - Commented out: pyserini (installed separately), pylate (Python 3.13 incompatible), qwen-agent (conflicts with vLLM)
     - Key content:
     ```
     openai>=1.97.0
     anthropic>=0.58.2
     google-genai>=1.27.0
     google-auth>=2.40.3
     faiss-cpu>=1.11.0.post1
     fastmcp==2.9.2
     pyngrok>=7.2.12
     pandas
     numpy
     matplotlib>=3.9.0
     seaborn>=0.13.0
     tqdm>=4.67.1
     python-dotenv>=1.1.1
     rich>=14.0.0
     gspread>=6.2.1
     ```

   - **bash/docker/build.bash**
     - Builds Docker image with tag browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0
     - Key snippet:
     ```bash
     IMAGE_NAME="${IMAGE_NAME:-browsecomp-neuron}"
     IMAGE_TAG="${IMAGE_TAG:-0.21.0}"
     FULL_IMAGE_NAME="${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
     
     cd "$REPO_ROOT"
     docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t "$FULL_IMAGE_NAME" .
     ```

   - **bash/docker/run.bash**
     - Launches interactive container with fixed name
     - Auto-detects and mounts all Neuron devices
     - Key snippet:
     ```bash
     CONTAINER_NAME="${CONTAINER_NAME:-browsecomp-neuron}"
     
     # Auto-detect Neuron devices
     NEURON_DEVICES=()
     for device in /dev/neuron*; do
         if [[ -c "$device" ]]; then
             NEURON_DEVICES+=("--device=$device:$device")
         fi
     done
     
     docker run -it \
         --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" \
         --network host \
         --privileged \
         "${NEURON_DEVICES[@]}" \
         -v "$REPO_ROOT:/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus" \
         "$FULL_IMAGE_NAME"
     ```

   - **~/.bashrc** (modified)
     - Changed VLLM_CACHE_ROOT from `/dev3/zigeng/vLLM_cache` to `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache`
     - This ensures cache is within mounted project directory

   - **bash/neuron/*.bash scripts** (8 files modified)
     - Added Docker environment auto-detection to all scripts using PYTHON_BIN
     - Pattern applied to: serve_tongyi.bash, serve_vllm_neuron.bash, compile_qwen3_32b_judge.bash, serve_qwen3_embedding.bash, run_tongyi_neuron.bash, n_tongyi.bash, n_tongyi_get.bash, evaluate_with_neuron_judge.bash
     - Key pattern:
     ```bash
     # Detect if running in Docker container
     if [[ -f /.dockerenv ]] || grep -q docker /proc/1/cgroup 2>/dev/null; then
         PYTHON_BIN="${PYTHON_BIN:-python3}"
     else
         PYTHON_BIN="${PYTHON_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/.venv-neuron/bin/python}"
     fi
     ```

   - **DOCKER_MIGRATION_README.md**
     - Complete migration guide documenting the entire Docker setup
     - Includes quick start, troubleshooting, comparison tables, and workflows

   - **docker/install_optional_deps.sh**
     - Script to install optional dependencies if they failed during build
     - Currently checks and installs pyserini if missing

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Error 1: Pyserini download timeout during Docker build**
     - Symptom: Connection reset while downloading 166MB pyserini-2.0.0.tar.gz
     - User feedback: No direct response, error occurred during build
     - Fix: Modified Dockerfile to install pyserini separately with longer timeout and retries, with fallback to manual installation in container
     - Code change: Split into separate RUN command with `--retries 5 --timeout 300` and `|| true` to not fail build

   - **Error 2: pylate[voyager] dependency conflict with Python 3.13**

# BrowseComp-Plus Docker Dependencies
# Only includes packages NOT provided by AWS Neuron DLC
# DLC already provides: torch, transformers, datasets, vllm, neuron-sdk

# API clients for agent evaluation
openai>=1.97.0
anthropic>=0.58.2
google-genai>=1.27.0
google-auth>=2.40.3

# Retrieval dependencies
faiss-cpu>=1.11.0.post1
# pyserini>=1.2.0  # BM25 (requires Java 21) - Installed separately due to size
# pylate>=1.4.0  # Dense retrieval - Temporarily disabled for Python 3.13 compatibility

# Qwen agent framework
# Note: Temporarily disabled due to Python 3.13 compatibility issues
# Full native install: qwen-agent[gui,mcp,rag]==0.0.27
# qwen-agent[mcp,rag]==0.0.27
# qwen-omni-utils==0.0.8

# MCP server
fastmcp==2.9.2
pyngrok>=7.2.12

# Data processing & visualization
pandas
numpy
matplotlib>=3.9.0
seaborn>=0.13.0
tqdm>=4.67.1

# Utilities
python-dotenv>=1.1.1
rich>=14.0.0
gspread>=6.2.1

# Note: The following are likely already in DLC but kept for safety
# - datasets>=4.0.0
# - transformers>=4.53.2
# - accelerate>=1.9.0
# - peft>=0.16.0
# - torchvision

# Git dependencies (install separately after base packages)
# tevatron @ git+https://github.com/texttron/tevatron.git@main

#!/bin/bash
# Install optional dependencies that may have failed during image build
# Run this inside the container if you need these packages

set -e

echo "Installing optional dependencies..."

# Check if pyserini is installed
if python3 -c "import pyserini" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "✓ pyserini already installed"
else
    echo "Installing pyserini (this may take a while, ~160MB download)..."
    pip install --retries 5 --timeout 300 pyserini>=1.2.0
    echo "✓ pyserini installed"
fi

echo "All optional dependencies installed successfully!"

# BrowseComp-Plus Docker Image
# Based on AWS Neuron vLLM Inference Container

FROM public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.21.0.1.0.0-neuronx-py313-sdk2.31.0-ubuntu24.04

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        openjdk-21-jdk \
        git \
        curl \
        wget \
        vim \
        tmux \
        htop \
        tree \
        less \
        && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set JAVA_HOME for Pyserini
ENV JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64

# Copy and install Python dependencies
COPY docker/requirements.txt /tmp/requirements.txt

# Install most dependencies (excluding large packages)
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r /tmp/requirements.txt && \
    rm /tmp/requirements.txt

# Install large packages separately with retries to handle network issues
# Pyserini is ~160MB and may timeout
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --retries 5 --timeout 300 pyserini>=1.2.0 || \
    (echo "Pyserini installation failed, will retry on container start" && true)

# Install git dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir git+https://github.com/texttron/tevatron.git@main

# Copy and setup entrypoint
COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

# BrowseComp-Plus Docker Migration Guide

## Overview

This document describes the migration from native Neuron vLLM setup to a Docker-based deployment using AWS Neuron Deep Learning Containers (DLC) with vLLM 0.21.0.

**Migration Date:** 2026-07-21  
**vLLM Version:** 0.21.0.1.0.0  
**Neuron SDK:** 2.31.0  
**Python Version:** 3.13  
**Base Image:** `public.ecr.aws/neuron/pytorch-inference-vllm-neuronx:0.21.0.1.0.0-neuronx-py313-sdk2.31.0-ubuntu24.04`

## Why Migrate?

1. **New vLLM-Neuron Release**: vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 introduces improvements and is the recommended version from AWS.
2. **Simplified Environment Management**: DLC provides pre-configured, tested Neuron stack eliminating manual SDK setup.
3. **Clean Slate**: Start fresh with new compilation approach and configurations based on latest best practices.
4. **Reproducibility**: Docker ensures consistent environment across development and production.

## Architecture Changes

### Before (Native)
```
Host Machine
├── .venv-neuron/          # Neuron Python environment
├── .venv/                 # Client Python environment  
├── local-models/          # Models and compiled artifacts
└── bash/neuron/*.bash     # Native Neuron scripts
```

### After (Docker)
```
Host Machine
├── docker/
│   ├── Dockerfile         # Custom image definition
│   ├── entrypoint.sh      # Container initialization
│   └── requirements.txt   # Project dependencies
├── bash/docker/
│   ├── build.bash         # Image build script
│   └── run.bash           # Container launch script
└── local-models/          # Mounted into container

Docker Container (browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0)
├── DLC Python 3.13        # Pre-installed vLLM-Neuron
├── Project dependencies   # Installed from requirements.txt
└── /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/  # Mounted from host
```

**Key Principle:** All code execution (clients, servers, retrievers, evaluation) happens **inside the container**. The container mounts the project directory, so code changes on the host are immediately visible.

## File Structure

### New Files Created

```
docker/
├── Dockerfile              # Custom image built on top of DLC
├── entrypoint.sh          # Sets environment variables on container start
└── requirements.txt       # Project-specific Python dependencies

bash/docker/
├── build.bash             # Build Docker image
└── run.bash               # Launch interactive container
```

### Modified Files

**Environment Configuration:**
- `~/.bashrc`: Changed `VLLM_CACHE_ROOT` to `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/vllm-cache`

**Bash Scripts (Auto-detect Docker):**
- `bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash`
- `bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash`
- `bash/neuron/compile_qwen3_32b_judge.bash`
- `bash/neuron/serve_qwen3_embedding.bash`
- `bash/neuron/run_tongyi_neuron.bash`
- `bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash`
- `bash/neuron/n_tongyi_get.bash`
- `bash/neuron/evaluate_with_neuron_judge.bash`

All scripts now detect if running in Docker and use `python3` instead of `.venv-neuron/bin/python`.

## Quick Start

### 1. Build the Docker Image

```bash
bash bash/docker/build.bash
```

This creates the `browsecomp-neuron:0.21.0` image with:
- vLLM-Neuron 0.21.0 + Neuron SDK 2.31.0 (from DLC)
- Java 21 (for Pyserini BM25)
- Project dependencies (OpenAI, Anthropic, FAISS, qwen-agent, etc.)
- Development tools (git, vim, tmux, htop)

Build time: ~5-10 minutes (depending on network and package installation).

### 2. Launch Interactive Container

```bash
bash bash/docker/run.bash
```

This starts a container with:
- **Name:** `browsecomp-neuron` (fixed, only one can run at a time)
- **Network:** Host mode (services accessible via `localhost`)
- **Devices:** All `/dev/neuron*` devices mounted
- **Volumes:** Project directory mounted at `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`
- **Working Directory:** `/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus`

You'll enter an interactive bash shell inside the container.

### 3. Run Neuron Scripts

Inside the container, all existing bash scripts work without modification:

```bash
# Compile Tongyi
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash

# Serve Tongyi
bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash

# Run full benchmark
bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
```

Scripts automatically detect they're in Docker and use the correct Python (`python3` from DLC).

## Recommended Workflow: Tmux Integration

### Setup

1. **On Host:** Start a tmux session
2. **In Tmux Pane:** Launch the interactive container
3. **From Host/Claude Code:** Send commands to the tmux pane

```bash
# In tmux pane 0
bash bash/docker/run.bash

# The container starts in /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus
# You're now inside the container
```

### Claude Code Integration

Claude Code can send commands to the tmux pane where your Docker container is running:

```bash
# Claude Code executes this on the host
tmux send-keys -t 0 "bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash" Enter
```

The command executes inside the container. Since the project directory is mounted, code changes made by Claude Code on the host are immediately visible inside the container.

### Benefits

- **Interactive debugging:** Manually attach to tmux to inspect running processes
- **Persistent sessions:** Container keeps running even if you detach from tmux
- **Claude Code automation:** Automate container operations via tmux commands
- **Code hot-reload:** Edit code on host, run inside container instantly

## Container Management

### Check Container Status

```bash
docker ps -a | grep browsecomp-neuron
```

### Stop Container

```bash
docker stop browsecomp-neuron
```

### Remove Container

```bash
docker rm browsecomp-neuron
```

### Restart Container

```bash
# If container exists but is stopped
docker start -ai browsecomp-neuron

# Or remove and recreate
docker rm browsecomp-neuron
bash bash/docker/run.bash
```

### View Container Logs (if detached)

```bash
docker logs -f browsecomp-neuron
```

### Execute Command in Running Container

```bash
docker exec -it browsecomp-neuron bash
docker exec browsecomp-neuron neuron-ls
```

## Environment Variables

The `docker/entrypoint.sh` automatically sets these on container start:

```bash
HF_TOKEN=[REDACTED] variables can be added to `.env` in the project root (loaded automatically by entrypoint).

## Compiled Artifacts Compatibility

**Good News:** Compiled artifacts from native setup are **compatible** with Docker setup, provided:
- Same Neuron SDK version (2.31.0)
- Same vLLM version (0.21.0)
- Same model configuration (TP size, max_len, etc.)

Existing artifacts in `local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/` will be reused by the Docker container. If versions differ, new artifacts will be compiled automatically.

## Comparison: Native vs Docker

| Aspect | Native Setup | Docker Setup |
|--------|--------------|--------------|
| **Python** | `.venv-neuron` (3.10/3.12) | DLC Python 3.13 |
| **vLLM** | Manually installed | Pre-installed 0.21.0 |
| **Neuron SDK** | Manually installed | Pre-installed 2.31.0 |
| **Environment** | Shared host packages | Isolated container |
| **Startup** | Instant | +1-2s container overhead |
| **Code Changes** | Direct | Via mounted volume (instant) |
| **Artifact Location** | `local-models/` | `local-models/` (mounted) |
| **Artifact Compatibility** | ✅ Version-dependent | ✅ Version-dependent |
| **Debugging** | Direct access | Via `docker exec` or tmux |

## Dependencies

### What's in the DLC (Pre-installed)

- PyTorch (Neuron-optimized)
- vLLM 0.21.0 with Neuron plugin
- Neuron SDK 2.31.0
- transformers, datasets, accelerate
- Basic Python libraries (numpy, etc.)

### What We Add (requirements.txt)

- **API Clients:** openai, anthropic, google-genai
- **Retrieval:** faiss-cpu, pyserini, pylate
- **Agent Framework:** qwen-agent, qwen-omni-utils
- **MCP:** fastmcp, pyngrok
- **Data & Viz:** pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, tqdm
- **Utilities:** python-dotenv, rich, gspread

### Git Dependencies (Installed Separately)

- `tevatron` - Dense retrieval framework

## Troubleshooting

### Image Build Fails

**Symptom:** `docker build` fails with package installation errors

**Solutions:**
1. Check network connectivity to PyPI and GitHub
2. Ensure `docker/requirements.txt` has valid package versions
3. Check available disk space (`df -h`)

### Container Can't See Neuron Devices

**Symptom:** `neuron-ls` returns no devices inside container

**Solutions:**
1. Verify devices exist on host: `ls -l /dev/neuron*`
2. Check `bash/docker/run.bash` includes `--device` mounts
3. Try `--privileged` mode (already enabled in run.bash)

### Python Module Not Found

**Symptom:** `ModuleNotFoundError` when running scripts

**Solutions:**
1. Check if package is in `docker/requirements.txt`
2. Rebuild image: `bash bash/docker/build.bash`
3. Verify `PYTHONPATH` is set: `echo $PYTHONPATH` (should include project root)

### Compilation Fails with Memory Error

**Symptom:** `NCC_EOOM002` during compilation

**Solutions:**
1. Use `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=1` to serialize compilation
2. Increase systemd memory limits (already set to 1750G/1900G in scripts)
3. Compile one model at a time

### Port Already in Use

**Symptom:** `Address already in use` when starting vLLM server

**Solutions:**
1. Check if server is already running: `curl localhost:6008/v1/models`
2. Use different port: `PORT=6009 bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash`
3. Stop existing server

### Container Exits Immediately

**Symptom:** `docker ps` shows no running container

**Solutions:**
1. Check container logs: `docker logs browsecomp-neuron`
2. Verify entrypoint script has execute permissions
3. Check for syntax errors in `docker/entrypoint.sh`

## Migration Checklist

- [x] Created `docker/Dockerfile` with DLC base image
- [x] Created `docker/entrypoint.sh` with environment setup
- [x] Created `docker/requirements.txt` with project dependencies
- [x] Created `bash/docker/build.bash` for image building
- [x] Created `bash/docker/run.bash` for container launching
- [x] Updated `~/.bashrc` to point caches to project directory
- [x] Modified all Neuron bash scripts to auto-detect Docker
- [x] Documented migration in this README

## Next Steps

1. **Build and Test:**
   ```bash
   bash bash/docker/build.bash
   bash bash/docker/run.bash
   # Inside container:
   neuron-ls
   python3 --version
   ```

2. **Smoke Test Compilation:**
   ```bash
   # Inside container:
   MAX_QUERIES=1 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash --compile-only
   ```

3. **Run Benchmark:**
   ```bash
   # Inside container:
   bash bash/neuron/n_tongyi.bash
   ```

4. **Clean Up Old Environments (Optional):**
   ```bash
   # On host, after verifying Docker setup works
   rm -rf .venv-neuron/
   rm -rf .venv-neuron-embedding/
   ```

## References

- [vLLM-Neuron Setup Guide](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-neuron/blob/release-0.21.0.1.0.0/docs/getting-started/setup-guide.md)
- [AWS Neuron DLC Repository](https://github.com/aws-neuron/deep-learning-containers)
- [Original Neuron Documentation](docs/neuron_trn2.md)

## Support

For issues specific to this migration, check:
1. This migration guide
2. `docs/neuron_trn2.md` for Neuron-specific configuration
3. `CLAUDE.md` for project conventions

skills.name: grill-with-docs
skills.content:
Base directory for this skill: /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/.claude/skills/grill-with-docs

<what-to-do>

Interview me relentlessly about every aspect of this plan until we reach a shared understanding. Walk down each branch of the design tree, resolving dependencies between decisions one-by-one. For each question, provide your recommended answer.

Ask the questions one at a time, waiting for feedback on each question before continuing.

If a question can be answered by exploring the codebase, explore the codebase instead.

</what-to-do>

<supporting-info>

## Domain awareness

During codebase exploration, also look for existing documentation:

### File structure

Most repos have a single context:

```
/
├── CONTEXT.md
├── docs/
│   └── adr/
│       ├── 0001-event-sourced-orders.md
│       └── 0002-postgres-for-write-model.md
└── src/
```

If a `CONTEXT-MAP.md` exists at the root, the repo has multiple contexts. The map points to where each one lives:

```
/
├── CONTEXT-MAP.md
├── docs/
│   └── adr/                          ← system-wide decisions
├── src/
│   ├── ordering/
│   │   ├── CONTEXT.md
│   │   └── docs/adr/                 ← context-specific decisions
│   └── billing/
│       ├── CONTEXT.md
│       └── docs/adr/
```

Create files lazily — only when you have something to write. If no `CONTEXT.md` exists, create one when the first term is resolved. If no `docs/adr/` exists, create it when the first ADR is needed.

## During the session

### Challenge against the glossary

When the user uses a term that conflicts with the existing language in `CONTEXT.md`, call it out immediately. "Your glossary defines 'cancellation' as X, but you seem to mean Y — which is it?"

### Sharpen fuzzy language

When the user uses vague or overloaded terms, propose a precise canonical term. "You're saying 'account' — do you mean the Customer or the User? Those are different things."

### Discuss concrete scenarios

When domain relationships are being discussed, stress-test them with specific scenarios. Invent scenarios that probe edge cases and force the user to be precise about the boundaries between concepts.

### Cross-reference with code

When the user states how something works, check whether the code agrees. If you find a contradiction, surface it: "Your code cancels entire Orders, but you just said partial cancellation is possible — which is right?"

### Update CONTEXT.md inline

When a term is resolved, update `CONTEXT.md` right there. Don't batch these up — capture them as they happen. Use the format in [CONTEXT-FORMAT.md](./CONTEXT-FORMAT.md).

`CONTEXT.md` should be totally devoid of implementation details. Do not treat `CONTEXT.md` as a spec, a scratch pad, or a repository for implementation decisions. It is a glossary and nothing else.

### Offer ADRs sparingly

Only offer to create an ADR when all three are true:

1. **Hard to reverse** — the cost of changing your mind later is meaningful
2. **Surprising without context** — a future reader will wonder "why did they do it this way?"
3. **The result of a real trade-off** — there were genuine alternatives and you picked one for specific reasons

If any of the three is missing, skip the ADR. Use the format in [ADR-FORMAT.md](./ADR-FORMAT.md).

</supporting-info>

  我想根据这个新的方法通过docker完全推倒之前的neuron适配代码和脚本以及编译产物，使用新方法运行。所有的config让我来设置

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

340	    local urls=()
341	    local url
342	    IFS=, read -r -a urls <<< "$raw_urls"
343	    for url in "${urls[@]}"; do
344	        wait_for_server "" "${url%/}/models"
345	    done
346	}

352	#         refer) echo "$RUN_ROOT/tongyi_res_refer" ;;

362	#     esac

365	start_tongyi_server() {
366	    if [[ "$START_SERVER" != "1" ]]; then
367	        wait_for_planning_endpoints
368	        wait_for_server "" "http://127.0.0.1:${EMBEDDING_PORT}/health"
369	        return
370	    fi
371	
372	    EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="--no-async-scheduling${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:+ $EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS}" \
373	    MODEL="$MODEL" \
374	    FIRST_PORT="$PORT" \
375	    TONGYI_REPLICAS="$TONGYI_REPLICAS" \
376	    EMBEDDING_PORT="$EMBEDDING_PORT" \
377	    TP_SIZE="$TP_SIZE" \
378	    MAX_MODEL_LEN="$MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
379	    MAX_NUM_SEQS="$MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
380	    NEURON_SCHEDULER="$NEURON_SCHEDULER" \
381	    NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS" \
382	    LOG_DIR="$PLANNING_LOG_DIR" \
383	    PLANNING_RESTART_DIR="$PLANNING_RESTART_DIR" \
384	        bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi_cluster.bash" &
385	    TONGYI_PID=$!
386	
387	    for ((replica = 0; replica < TONGYI_REPLICAS; replica++)); do
388	        wait_for_server \
389	            "$TONGYI_PID" \
390	            "http://127.0.0.1:$((PORT + replica))/v1/models"
391	    done
392	    wait_for_server "$TONGYI_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${EMBEDDING_PORT}/health"
393	
394	    if [[ "$ENABLE_TP8_SIDECAR" == "1" ]]; then
395	        local sidecar_log_dir="${TP8_SIDECAR_LOG_DIR:-$PLANNING_LOG_DIR}"
396	        local sidecar_log="$sidecar_log_dir/tongyi-${TP8_SIDECAR_PORT}.log"
397	        mkdir -p "$sidecar_log_dir"
398	        echo "Starting TP8 single-sequence sidecar: port=$TP8_SIDECAR_PORT cores=$TP8_SIDECAR_CORES log=$sidecar_log"
399	        numactl --cpunodebind=1 --membind=1 \
400	            env \
401	            MODEL="$MODEL" \
402	            PORT="$TP8_SIDECAR_PORT" \
403	            TP_SIZE=8 \
404	            MAX_MODEL_LEN=128000 \
405	            MAX_NUM_SEQS=1 \
406	            NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE=4000 \
407	            NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$TP8_SIDECAR_CORES" \
408	            NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$TP8_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT" \
409	            NEURON_REQUIRE_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS=1 \
410	            NEURON_SCHEDULER=custom \
411	            ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=0 \
412	            OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG="" \
413	            NEURON_CACHE_TAG=tongyi-128k-prefix112-tp8-s1 \
414	            EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="${TP8_SIDECAR_EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
415	            bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi_tp8_s1.bash" \
416	            >"$sidecar_log" 2>&1 &
417	        TP8_SIDECAR_PID=$!
418	        wait_for_server "$TP8_SIDECAR_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${TP8_SIDECAR_PORT}/v1/models"
419	    fi
420	
421	    if [[ "$ENABLE_TP4_SIDECARS" == "1" ]]; then
422	        local sidecar_ports=("$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_1" "$TP4_SIDECAR_PORT_2")
423	        local sidecar_cores=("48-51" "52-55")
424	        local index sidecar_port sidecar_cores_for_port sidecar_log
425	
426	        mkdir -p "$PLANNING_LOG_DIR"
427	        for index in "${!sidecar_ports[@]}"; do
428	            sidecar_port="${sidecar_ports[$index]}"
429	            sidecar_cores_for_port="${sidecar_cores[$index]}"
430	            sidecar_log="$PLANNING_LOG_DIR/tongyi-${sidecar_port}.log"
431	            echo "Starting experimental TP4 single-sequence sidecar: port=$sidecar_port cores=$sidecar_cores_for_port log=$sidecar_log"
432	            numactl --cpunodebind=1 --membind=1 \
433	                env \
434	                MODEL="$MODEL" \
435	                PORT="$sidecar_port" \
436	                TP_SIZE=4 \
437	                MAX_MODEL_LEN=128000 \
438	                MAX_NUM_SEQS=1 \
439	                NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE=4000 \
440	                NEURON_RT_VISIBLE_CORES="$sidecar_cores_for_port" \
441	                NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$TP4_SIDECAR_ARTIFACT" \
442	                NEURON_REQUIRE_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS=1 \
443	                NEURON_SCHEDULER=custom \
444	                ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=0 \
445	                OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG='{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":128000,"max_context_length":128000,"context_encoding_buckets":[8192],"prefix_buckets":[122880],"token_generation_buckets":[16384,32768,65536,98304,128000],"save_sharded_checkpoint":true,"blockwise_matmul_config":{"use_shard_on_intermediate_dynamic_while":true}}' \
446	                NEURON_CACHE_TAG=tongyi-128k-prefix120-tp4-s1 \
447	                EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS="${TP4_SIDECAR_EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
448	                bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" \
449	                >"$sidecar_log" 2>&1 &
450	            TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS+=("$!")
451	            wait_for_server "${TP4_SIDECAR_PIDS[$index]}" "http://127.0.0.1:${sidecar_port}/v1/models"
452	        done
453	    fi
454	}
455	
456	start_judge_server() {
457	    if [[ "$START_JUDGE_SERVER" != "1" ]]; then

469	    JUDGE_PID=$!
470

472	    wait_for_server "$JUDGE_PID" "http://127.0.0.1:${JUDGE_PORT}/v1/models"
473	}

477	    local qrel_dir
478	    local qrel_label
479	    qrel_dir="$(qrel_dir_for_run "$qrel_run")"
480	    qrel_label="$(qrel_label_for_run "$qrel_run")"

483	    local max_query_args=()

485	    local restart_args=()

487

493	    fi

495	        restart_args=(
496	            --planning-restart-dir "$PLANNING_RESTART_DIR"
497	            --planning-restart-timeout-seconds "$PLANNING_RESTART_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"
498	            --planning-query-retry-limit "$PLANNING_QUERY_RETRY_LIMIT"
499	        )
500	    fi
501	    if [[ -n "$PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS" ]]; then
502	        prefix_guard_args=(--planning-max-uncached-prefill-tokens "$PLANNING_MAX_UNCACHED_PREFILL_TOKENS")
503	    fi
504	
505	    echo "Running filter_all Tongyi on $qrel_label without get_document on planning ports $PLANNING_PORTS"
506	    echo "Neuron embedding endpoint: http://127.0.0.1:$EMBEDDING_PORT"
507	    "$PYTHON_BIN" search_agent/tongyi_client.py \
508	        --model "$MODEL" \
509	        --query "$qrel_dir/queries.tsv" \
510	        --output-dir "$output_dir" \
511	        --cache-dir "$cache_dir" \
512	        --searcher-type faiss \
513	        --index-path "indexes/qwen3-embedding-8b/corpus.shard*.pkl" \
514	        --model-name "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B" \
515	        --normalize \

517	        --embedding-base-url "http://127.0.0.1:$EMBEDDING_PORT" \
518	        --embedding-timeout 60 \
519	        --max-length 1024 \
520	        --planning-ports "$PLANNING_PORTS" \
521	        --planning-endpoint-capacity "$PLANNING_ENDPOINT_CAPACITY" \
522	        "${endpoint_capacity_args[@]}" \
523	        --planning-lease-scope "$PLANNING_LEASE_SCOPE" \
524	        --planning-affinity-timeout "$PLANNING_AFFINITY_TIMEOUT" \
525	        "${restart_args[@]}" \
526	        "${prefix_guard_args[@]}" \
527	        --snippet-max-tokens 512 \
528	        --k 5 \
529	        --port "$PORT" \
530	        --qrel-evidence-path "$qrel_dir/qrel_evidence.txt" \
531	        --qrel-golds-path "$qrel_dir/qrel_golds.txt" \
532	        --filter-all \
533	        "${max_query_args[@]}"
534	}
